meta.name: 0 and X
meta.desc: 0 y X es un juego de tres en raya en tres dimensiones en un cubo de 4x4x4. Solo tienes que llenar una fila, columna o diagonal completa con X, ¡en cualquier dimensión!

meta.name: 1.000 Miles
meta.desc: "1000 Miles" se basa en el juego de cartas francés Mille Bornes. El jugador compite contra la computadora para alcanzar las 1000 millas primero. Cada jugador recibe siete cartas, que pueden ser cartas de millas para acercarse a su objetivo, o cartas positivas o negativas. Las cartas negativas pueden usarse para provocar un pinchazo en el oponente, provocar un choque u otros efectos. Las cartas positivas pueden usarse para contrarrestar los efectos de las cartas negativas que el oponente jugó contra usted.

meta.name: 17 und 04
meta.desc: "17 und 04" es un juego de Blackjack que solo se puede jugar en alemán.

meta.name: 1k-Mini-Bdash
meta.desc: "1k-Mini-Bdash" es un clon compacto de "Boulder Dash", programado en tan solo 1024 bytes. Al igual que en el original, los jugadores deben recolectar diamantes mientras evitan las rocas que caen y las luciérnagas mortales. Solo se puede salir de un nivel tras reunir la cantidad necesaria de diamantes. Según el desarrollador, el juego cuenta con un total de 1024 niveles diferentes.

meta.name: 3D Construction Kit
meta.desc:
Este software permite a los usuarios crear sus propios mundos en 3D, utilizando tecnología relacionada con el universo Freescape que se encuentra en juegos como Castle Master y Driller / Space Station Oblivion. Los menús desplegables permiten crear y colocar objetos.
Se incluye un sencillo lenguaje BASIC para permitir la creación de puzzles y condiciones (abrir una puerta cuando se coloca una llave en ella, por ejemplo). También es posible incluir enemigos a los que hay que disparar. En cualquier momento se puede entrar en el mundo del juego para probarlo.

meta.name: 3D Glooper
meta.desc: 3-D Glooper es una versión de Pac-Man donde debes recorrer una ciudad laberíntica comiendo bloques azules mientras intentas evitar a los Gloopers, los enemigos que la deambulan. Solo puedes escapar cuando todos los bloques hayan desaparecido. De vez en cuando, aparecerá un bloque rojo que te permitirá comerte a los Gloopers durante un breve periodo.

meta.name: 3D Glooper

meta.name: 3D Pool
meta.desc: 3D Pool es uno de los primeros juegos de billar en 3D, en el que el jugador puede girar alrededor de la mesa y tener un punto de vista variable que permite realizar tiros mucho más realistas que el estilo tradicional de juego desde arriba (aunque el juego puede jugarse de esta forma). Se puede ajustar el ritmo, el efecto y el ángulo de cada tiro. El juego incluye el juego de 8 bolas (los siete colores más el negro están en juego para ganar) y el juego de 9 bolas (el jugador siempre debe golpear primero la bola con el número más bajo que aún esté sobre la mesa).

meta.name: 3D Sakk
meta.desc: A three-dimensional game reminiscent of chess but with slightly altered rules for the movement of pawns. The chessboard itself is a grid-like spatial object, and the player has only five types of figures at his disposal. In general, the pawns must be used to surround the king in such a way that the king has no way to escape or knock down another pawn.

meta.name: 3D Time Trek
meta.desc:
Piratas espaciales lanzaron un devastador ataque contra el planeta Corillian. Has sobrevivido y ahora buscas vengarte de los responsables, destruirlos y acabar con su tiranía galáctica. 
Viaja por la galaxia en tu nave con la ayuda de un mapa. Tendrás que enviar sondas a distintas zonas para averiguar si hay alienígenas o planetas. Una vez exploradas las zonas, tendrás que continuar tu investigación en otro lugar. Mientras viajas a otro sector, puedes comprobar tu ordenador de batalla, recargar tus escudos y reparar tu nave.
El juego se desarrolla en 3D desde una perspectiva en primera persona. Desde la cabina del piloto, miras hacia las oscuras profundidades del espacio. Si te encuentras con un alienígena, aparece una cruz que te permite apuntar y dispararle.

meta.name: A Bolcsek Kove
meta.desc:
In an distant kingdom where people always lived in happiness and peace, a cunning and cruel monster, Zaalro, appeared. He decided to bring everything and everyone under his control, and to extend his dark empire to all the great shores of the continent.
The natives were powerless against his military, as they had never made weapons. However, a scientist named Vang Tu found the Stone of Sages and decided to use the knowledge he gained to overthrow Zaalro's rule. However, he was killed before he could carry out his plan.
Will you, the descendant of Vang Tu, manage to find the stone before the terrible warriors of Zaalro destroy it?

meta.name: Aardvark
meta.desc: Basado en el juego de arcade Anteater, eres un oso hormiguero y debes enterrar tu largo hocico en el hormiguero y recolectar las larvas de hormiga para pasar al siguiente hormiguero. Come hormigas que caminan por la pantalla antes de que te dañen el hocico y pierdas una de tus vidas. Ataca a la hormiga reina para destruir a todas las hormigas y acércate sigilosamente detrás de los gusanos para comerlos. Cuando el sol se pone, aparecerá una araña y te seguirá por donde te lleva el hocico.

Aunque la mayoría de los controles se pueden lograr con el joystick, el uso del teclado es esencial. Es posible jugar con dos personas: un piloto y un tirador.

meta.name: ACE 2
meta.desc: ACE 2 es la secuela de "ACE" y ofrece una simulación de combate aéreo llena de acción ambientada en un entorno moderno. Las misiones están definidas de forma general y se centran en derribar aeronaves enemigas. El juego tiene un marcado aire arcade, ya que el avión controlado es un hipotético caza moderno. Una novedad es la posibilidad de jugar en solitario contra la consola o en pantalla dividida contra un segundo jugador.

meta.name: Aces Up
meta.desc: Aces Up te enfrenta al caos de la baraja con un solo objetivo: dejar solo los cuatro ases en pie. Cada ronda reparte una nueva fila, y solo puedes descartar cartas de menor valor y del mismo palo. Parece fácil, pero rápidamente se convierte en un tenso rompecabezas de movimientos bloqueados, decisiones inteligentes y momentos donde todo depende de una sola carta. Los modos de juego alternativos ofrecen variedad, pero la frustración persiste cuando ese último as yace enterrado bajo una montaña de oportunidades perdidas.

meta.name: Ack Ack Attack
Adventure
meta.desc: Adventure is a classic text adventure where you navigate a mysterious world using simple typed commands. You begin in a small room with items like wallpaper and rope. Your task is to explore, interpret clues, and enter meaningful actions to solve puzzles and escape. Every move requires imagination, experimentation, and a feel for language.

meta.name: Zeittunnel - Flucht in die Gegenwart
meta.desc: Zeittunnel: Flucht in die Gegenwart te lanza desde tu apartamento a un extraño mundo de cristal: quizá un futuro, quizá otra dimensión. Las máquinas dominan el terreno, la vida escasea. Tu objetivo es volver a casa. La aventura de texto utiliza un analizador de dos palabras, alterna entre gráficos PETSCII y modo texto, pero no ofrece vista de guardado ni de inventario. Solo se necesitan dos objetos, pero la muerte acecha en formas extrañas y sangrientas. Debes encontrar tres códigos generados aleatoriamente para desbloquear el camino de regreso al presente.

meta.name: Adventureland : Adventure #01
meta.desc:
Adventureland es la primera de las aventuras de texto de Scott Adams. Usando comandos simples de dos palabras, explora un mundo encantado, resuelve acertijos e intenta recuperar 13 tesoros perdidos.
La visualización del juego es diferente de otros juegos de aventuras como Zork: The Great Underground Empire en que la pantalla está dividida en dos "ventanas": la descripción de la habitación, las salidas y los elementos aparecen en la parte superior de la pantalla, y tu tipeas comandos en la parte inferior de la pantalla.

meta.name: Adventures in Time
meta.desc: During a walk in the woods one day our hero, a curious yellow snorkel-shaking bouncy thing, discovered some ancient ruins. Inside the ruins he found something rather unexpected - an ultra-super modern TIME SCRAMBLING MACHINE. Being a curious little fellow our hero reached out to touch a tantalizing red button on the control panel ... A bright flash - and so begins his ADVENTURES IN TIME!

meta.name: AFL Boulder Dash
meta.desc: Otra variante de Boulder Dash programada por el grupo de cracking Alpha Flight para el C64.

meta.name: African Adventure : In Search of Dr. Livingston
meta.desc: Interpretas a un hombre que debe ayudar a buscar a un hombre llamado Dr. Livingston que se ha perdido en algún lugar de las partes más oscuras de África. Para cada acción que escribas, todas las letras deben estar en mayúsculas, y su declaración solo puede ser de dos palabras: un verbo y un sustantivo, como "get book" o "go west". Para obtener instrucciones, también puedes ingresar la primera letra de la palabra como por ejemplo e, para el este.Por los objetos que recoges ganas puntos. Algunos deben usarse en otros lugares, o deben conservarse a medida que avanza el juego.

meta.name: African Safari
meta.desc: Juegas el papel del Dr. Livingstone, quien debe explorar un parque nacional cerca del río Zambezi con la ayuda de un mono en su búsqueda del diamante keniano perdido hace mucho tiempo.

meta.name: Airwolf
meta.desc: Airwolf es un juego de acción en el que tomas el control de un helicóptero y debes rescatar a cinco científicos que están prisioneros en una guarida subterránea amenazada por el enemigo. Para avanzar en el juego, debes disparar a todo lo que se encuentra en tu camino. El juego, basado en la serie de televisión, se ve desde un lateral y se mueve de pantalla en pantalla. Además, debes tener cuidado de no quedarte quieto en una posición debido a la gravedad realista del juego. En la versión en alemán se menciona que el helicóptero es el Airwolf, valorado en miles de millones de dólares y que solo Hawke, un piloto militar retirado, puede pilotar. Su misión es rescatar a los científicos que han sido secuestrados en un sistema de cuevas subterráneas en el desierto de Arizona. El juego solo otorga un vida al jugador y el objetivo es recoger a los cinco científicos y llevarlos de regreso a la base.

meta.name: Alex
meta.desc: The knight Taksony kidnapped the daughter of King Willy. It's Alex's task to retrieve her. To do that, the player has to lead him through five scenes and protect him from the evil creatures standing ready to attack him.

meta.name: Alien
meta.desc: El juego sigue a la película, en el sentido de que al principio del juego un miembro del equipo (no siempre, pero a menudo Kane) da a luz? al extranjero. Luego depende del resto de la tripulación que controla el jugador moverse por los tres niveles del Nostromo usando los conductos de aire, intentando aislar al alienígena y destruirlo. O sácalo de la esclusa de aire del Nostromo, o como en la película, establece la autodestrucción en el barco y escapa en la cápsula de escape.

meta.name: Alien Attack
meta.desc: Alien Attack begins with what should be a peaceful farming job on planet Scelor. But after landing, a mothership opens a dimensional warp and sends waves of hostile droids. You quickly equip your agribots with lasers to fight back. The droids hover and drop smartbombs you can't shoot – only dodge. The mothership also releases birds that home in and drop slow bombs, which you can shoot. Your goal: destroy the mothership with a single laser blast and end the invasion. But first, you'll have to defeat guardian droids that change form three times before vanishing.

meta.name: Almatura
meta.desc: An infinitely voracious worm crawls through the labyrinth, and if an apple gets in its way, it will devour it. Each apple eaten adds a section to the worm's body. This makes it more and more difficult for him to navigate the labyrinth without distraction. It is always in danger of colliding with itself. If this happens, the game starts over.

meta.name: Ambush
meta.desc: Ambush puts you in control of a spaceship moving freely across a single screen while enemies descend from the top. They bounce off the screen edges and shift speed and direction. Shooting them causes them to split into smaller, faster pieces – sometimes more than once. There's no scrolling, no escape – just pure arcade chaos that keeps escalating.

meta.name: Amoba
meta.desc: "Amoba" is a strategy game where two players take turns placing their symbols on a grid. The goal is to align five of their own symbols in a row, column, or diagonal before the opponent does. The game requires foresight and skillful placement to maximize winning opportunities while blocking the opponent's strategies.

meta.name: Android Nim
meta.desc: In Android Nim, you play against the computer with the goal of removing the last android from the board. Each turn, a player removes any number of androids from a single row – at least one. The game begins with three rows of androids: seven, five, and three. Choose a row and a number, and a lively animated android zaps them away with a laser. Then it's the computer's turn. The game continues until the last android is gone – and whoever makes that final move is the winner.

meta.name: Asteroid !
meta.desc: In Asteroid ! you are tasked with saving a city on the planet Klinx in the Sirius system from a deadly swarm of asteroids. Using a laser cannon, you control a crosshair to destroy incoming rocks before they hit the dome city. Each destroyed asteroid earns you points, while missed ones cost you points. The dome can only withstand three hits before the game ends. Up to nine players can take turns, with each round being time-limited. Precision and quick reflexes are key to protecting the city.

meta.name: Astral Attack
meta.desc: In Astral Attack, you must protect six energy generators at the bottom of the screen from waves of attacking XENO aliens. When they hit energy lines, they transform into bombs and descend rapidly. You can only shoot them once they become bombs. Missing a shot may cost you a generator. When all generators are destroyed, the game ends. You control the crosshair with a joystick or, in the original version, with the Stack Light Rifle.

meta.name: Astro Plumber
meta.desc:
You play the game as a repair man equipped with a jetpack and an upwards firing plasma laser. Your mission is to repair all the leaking air pipes which supply the Colony research station on the surface of the Moon.
Sounds simple enough, but now the caverns are inhabited by a number of alien lifeforms which will kill you if they touch you.
Finally you must keep an eye on your ever decreasing air supply. You can refill from one of the big air tanks on the surface but it means breaking off your repairs. Your space suit tank can only hold so much air. Overfilling is deadly!

meta.name: Schlacht im Atlantik
meta.desc: Atlantic Patrol puts you in command of a German warship during World War II. Your mission is to patrol the seas and sink as many enemy ships as possible. The closer you get to your target, the more accurate your shots – but also the higher the risk of being hit. Before deployment, you choose your ship type: heavily armored battleship, agile cruiser, or fast but vulnerable destroyer. Strategic decisions, course changes, weapon control, and torpedo use are all key to dominating the seas.

meta.name: Atlantis
meta.desc: Atlantis is an adventure game in which you take on the role of an explorer. By chance, documents about the legendary missing city of Atlantis were found. A well-equipped group of scientists disappeared during the search. The irresistible urge to solve the mystery involved won't let you rest for a minute. Silently expecting fame and fortune, you sell everything you own to equip and finance a new expedition... The expedition begins in a boat on the sea near the ruins of the Sunken Culture. The first difficulty is to take the right equipment and find your way to the outer wall of Atlantis through various adversities such as endless currents and voracious squids. After you get into the labyrinth of passages and chambers (which is confusing to say the least), you have to find more valuables and pieces of equipment in order to convince the guardians of the untold treasure of this lost civilization to open the way.

meta.name: Atomic Mission
meta.desc: This is adventure #3 in Scott Adam's text adventure series. It takes place in a nuclear power plant. You must race against time to fulfill your mission or the reactor may be doomed. You use one or two word commands to move around and manipulate objects.

meta.name: Atomino
meta.desc: In Atomino you have to arrange atoms with each one to four free connections in a way so that stable molecules are created. The atoms fall with rising speed one after the other into a shaft, that offers space for 6 atoms, whereby the current atoms is always lowest. From there the atoms need to be placed on the game area. You can place altogether 56 atoms (7x8) there. If atoms with free connections lie next to each other they connect and as soon as a molecule without any free connections has been created, it is automatically removed from the game area. If the shaft for the atoms is more than full, the game is over.

meta.name: Atomix
meta.desc: "Atomix" is a puzzle game where the player must reconstruct a specific molecular structure in each level. The atoms are displayed from a top-down perspective, with a small preview of the target molecule shown at the bottom left. Atoms can only be moved horizontally or vertically and will not stop until they hit a wall or another atom. This requires clever use of obstacles to achieve the correct arrangement. The game consists of 30 levels, each with a time limit.

meta.name: AMC : Attack of the Mutant Camels
meta.desc: “AMC: Attack of the Mutant Camels” is a side-scrolling shooter where the player pilots a spaceship and fights off gigantic mutant camels charging toward the base. Unlike traditional side-scrollers, the ship can fly in both directions, and the jet speed is adjustable. The camels start out yellow and change color with each hit until they are ultimately destroyed. There's no time to waste, though – the enemies are relentlessly advancing and must be stopped quickly. In North America, the game was released as “Advance of the Mega Camels” to prevent confusion with other titles.

meta.name: Auf Wiedersehen Monty
meta.desc: Auf Wiedersehen Monty sends you across Europe with Monty as you collect money to finally buy your own Greek island and retire in peace. You move screen by screen through varied locations that playfully reference real-world landmarks and demand careful timing and awareness. Hazards, obstacles, and collectibles force you to explore each area methodically and plan your routes. The appeal lies in the blend of exploration, precise control, and the clear goal of turning Monty's dream into reality step by step.

meta.name: Auriga
meta.desc: Auriga turns the entrance test for a space-fighter squadron into a tough trial by fire against wave after wave of aliens. As an inexperienced pilot, you guide your ship through repeated attacks, dodge bombs, and shoot enemy formations out of the sky. After each cleared section, a risky docking sequence awaits, with falling meteorites creating the next danger. That mix of classic screen-based action, pressure to prove yourself, and short skill-based sequences gives the game its driving appeal.

meta.name: Automania : Manic Mechanic
meta.desc: Auto Mania is a platform game in which the player must help Wally, who works on a car assembly line, build a series of ten cars. Each car consists of six parts that are scattered throughout the storage room and must be collected in order to place them in the correct place on the car at the assembly point. The factory is populated with things that kill Wally on contact, such as robots, bouncing tires, and air cooling blades.

meta.name: Auto Zone
meta.desc:
"You are trapped on a supposedly dead moon and your only means of escape is to drive your small highly versatile surface buggy across the many treacherous sectors of the Auto Zone."
This is a horizontally scrolling platformer game, where the screen keeps scrolling automatically to the left as your vehicle (called the XL-Procon) is progressing forward. You have to guide the vehicle by making it jump over gaps and onto higher platforms to avoid collision with walls or fall into the water. There are two speeds you can travel with, so you can adjust your jumps to the appropriate situation. The game features 16 levels and colorful graphics comparable to Atari 8-bit standards.

This is a horizontally scrolling platformer game, where the screen keeps scrolling automatically to the left as your vehicle (called the XL-Procon) is progressing forward. You have to guide the vehicle by making it jump over gaps and onto higher platforms to avoid collision with walls or fall into the water. There are two speeds you can travel with, so you can adjust your jumps to the appropriate situation. The game features 16 levels and colorful graphics comparable to Atari 8-bit standards.

meta.name: The Game of Awari
meta.desc: The Game of Awari is a digital version of the classic African board game Kalaha. You play against the computer and must plan your moves carefully to capture as many seeds (or stones) as possible. Each of your six pits starts with four stones, which you distribute clockwise. The goal is to collect more stones in your store than your opponent. Strategy, foresight, and timing are key to winning – a simple yet deeply strategic game that rewards logic and keen observation.

meta.name: Aztec Tomb Adventure
meta.desc: In Aztec Tomb Adventure, you embark on a perilous expedition through the jungle to find the lost Aztec tomb. Along the way, you'll solve puzzles, escape a house, avoid hazards, cross a river, and explore an island. The game blends graphic scenes with simple text input, requiring you to navigate using commands like “go west” or “open door.” Only those with persistence, clever thinking, and keen observation will uncover the hidden tomb.

meta.name: Baby Berks
meta.desc: Your mission is to move around a single screen in a craft blasting all the Baby Berks over 16 levels. Most of the Berks are in an egg and you have to wait for them to hatch before you can blast them. Using your weapon though loses you energy shown by a gauge on the bottom of the screen. Big Berks move around as well but these can't be killed, just stunned. If you touch a Baby Berk, Big Berk, egg or run out of energy then you lose one of five lives. There are five skill levels to choose from and they are Duffer, Novice, Normal, Expert and Master.

meta.name: Backgammon
meta.desc: „Backgammon“ is a board game for two players, where the goal is to move all of your pieces (checkers) into your home board and eventually remove them from the board. Each player has 15 pieces, which are moved using two dice. The dice roll determines how many spaces a piece can be moved. The objective is to bring all pieces into your home board first, and then remove them from the board in a specific order. Obstacles include opponent's pieces, which can be "hit" and returned to the game. Tactics and risk play a significant role, as the game requires both luck and strategic thinking. The first player to remove all their pieces wins.

meta.name: Ball
meta.desc:
The aim of this game is to control a ball against the generic wall and, if necessary, deflect it. The program has a few nasty tricks up its sleeve. Another ball floats vertically up and down in the middle of the screen. When the cue ball touches the ball in question, our peaceful cue ball turns into a mean fellow whose only goal appears to be to send the player onto the boards. Among other things, lives can be deducted if the player's ball leaves the field of play, penalty points can be awarded, etc. If the counter above the scoreboard shows a 3, the game is over. The only chance is to be faster and more skilled than the computer to stay afloat. With a little practice, you too can outsmart the computer.
A final tip: By pressing the fire button you can move the racket faster - often very helpful in reaching the ball.

meta.name: Bandits At Zero
meta.desc: Bandits at Zero is a side view horizontal scrolling shooter. Your mission is to find and destroy an aircraft carrier somewhere out at sea over the course of a few days. The screen scrolls left or right constantly in the direction you are traveling. You must fly over the sea and survive every day and night until it is found while avoiding and shooting enemy planes. At the bottom of the screen is a radar which shows you the position of the enemy planes and as you fight the day slowly turns to night and back again. Flying decreases a fuel gauge slowly but once night turns back to day you have a chance to re-fuel with another plane by flying behind it. If you are shot by a plane or touch one, then you lose one of seven shields and if they run out or your fuel then it is game over.

meta.name: Barbarian
meta.desc: Barbarian pone al jugador en el papel de un guerrero que navega por peligrosas mazmorras. El juego se desarrolla desde una perspectiva de desplazamiento lateral y combina plataformas y combate. El joystick controla el movimiento, mientras que las teclas arriba y abajo te permiten navegar por los comandos en la parte inferior de la pantalla. Al pulsar "Disparo", se ejecuta la acción seleccionada, eliminando la necesidad de botones adicionales. El bárbaro empieza con una espada y más adelante adquiere un arco para ataques a distancia. Numerosas trampas y diversos monstruos con patrones de ataque únicos presentan desafíos constantes.

meta.name: Baseball
meta.desc: Baseball tries to bring the flow of a baseball game to the screen in a compact form, placing its main focus on the direct duels between pitcher and batter. Different pitch types, timing at the plate, and reacting correctly in the field ensure that success depends not only on speed, but also on a good feel for how each play unfolds. That focus on the key moments of the sport gives the program a simple but appealing sporting character.

meta.name: Big Mac
meta.desc:
In Big Mac, players must flip all the switches in a room and get to the exit in order to proceed to the next level. Various obstacles and puzzles stand in the way.
Though the box art refers to it as "More Adventures of Big Mac: The Mad Maintenance Man," the in-game title screen simply calls it "Big Mac: The Mad Maintenance Man."

meta.name: Black Thunder
meta.desc: In Black Thunder, you play as the superhuman Crow-Ther, fighting to escape the grip of The Wizard, who has spent a century preparing for your downfall. Driving an armored car loaded with cannons and missiles, you must navigate Wizening's massive highway system to survive. The top half of the screen shows your vehicle from the side, where you control speed and firepower. The bottom half offers a top-down map, letting you choose routes and junctions in real time. Deadly machines and remote-controlled androids pursue you relentlessly. You will return one day to defeat the tyrant – but first, you must survive.

meta.name: Blagger
meta.desc: En este juego, los jugadores controlan a Rodger the Dodger , un ladrón cuya tarea consiste en recoger una serie de llaves y utilizarlas para abrir una caja fuerte. Hay 30 niveles que completar. Blagger debe completar estas tareas en un tiempo limitado mientras evita peligros como cactus, coches, alienígenas, sombrereros locos, bocas gigantes y chinos. Blaguer no dispone de armas para disparar, sino que debe confiar en sus habilidades de salto. Pierde una vida si entra en contacto con un peligro, sufre una caída importante o se le acaba el tiempo. El juego se pierde cuando se acaban todas las vidas. Si Blagger consigue coger las llaves y dirigirse a la caja fuerte, pasa a la siguiente fase.

meta.name: Blaze
meta.desc: The ultimate test for any Space-Cadet of the Fleet is to fly through the Blaze Zone in the Andromeda System. The zone is packed full of defences and aliens that need shooting to hopefully emerge on the other side. The game is a shooter where you move a spacecraft on the bottom of the single screen left or right, and the aim is to shoot the various items and aliens that appear on the screen. You have to shoot everything on the screen to move to the next one. If you touch anything then you lose one of five lives.

meta.name: Blockbusters
meta.desc:
Bob Holness hosted this fondly-remembered quiz show, in which students competed to answer enough questions correctly to complete a run of answers across the board. This was the latest and most sophisticated computer version of the concept.
The exact mechanics of the game have been customized slightly. You start by choosing a question, which are all represented by the first letter of the answer. You must 'buzz in' by pressing your letter to input the answer (don't worry about exact spelling). If you get the question wrong, the computer chooses a square (seemingly with no strategy). In two player games each player has their own buzzer, and a correct answer sees you choose the next question. A digitized version of Bob's face reads out the questions - just be careful if you get any where the answer is 'organism'...

meta.name: Block Out
meta.desc: Block Out is a 3D twist on the classic Tetris concept. Instead of rotating blocks in just two dimensions, you must spin them in all directions from a top-down perspective to make them fit neatly into the pit without leaving gaps. The game challenges your spatial awareness, as you need to view and place blocks from different angles. Difficulty can be adjusted in the options by changing pit size, speed, rows, and more.

meta.name: BMX Racers
meta.desc: "BMX Racers" throws you into a wild ride through the park when suddenly your brakes fail – now it's just about dodging obstacles and holding on tight. Viewed from above, you must steer your bike along twisting paths, across rivers and past trees, fences, and parking lots full of cars. After each level you get a short break, but the next run is even faster and harder. Your score climbs as long as you stay on track – but one crash, and it's all over. The challenge: go the distance and beat your last score.

meta.name: Bmx Racers
meta.desc:
There you are having a nice ride through the park when suddenly you can't stop peddling and your brakes stop working. All you can do is avoid all obstacles and hope for the best as you get faster and faster.
The game is viewed from a overhead view as you cycle up the winding path. There are bridges to cross rivers, lakes, trees and bushes, fences and even car parks full of cars to avoid. After each level you get a small rest before once again your propelled forward, but this time much faster and the obstacles become more and more.
While all this is going on your score keeps getting bigger until you crash. You only have one life before you start all over again to get further and beat your last score.

meta.name: BMX Simulator
meta.desc: BMX Simulator te pone al manillar de una bici BMX en vista cenital, desafiándote en siete circuitos cada vez más técnicos, llenos de rampas y baches que ponen a prueba tu control. El objetivo: completar tres vueltas antes de que se agote el tiempo —cuanto más rápido, más puntos. Corres contra un rival de la IA, pero no es necesario ganarle: solo cuenta tu crono. Lo que sí brilla es una novedad de la época: la repetición en cámara lenta, para revivir (y presumir de) tus saltos, derrapes y aterrizajes al límite. Simple, ágil y adictivo: pura esencia BMX, sin florituras, con adrenalina en cada curva.

meta.name: Bobby Bearing
meta.desc: Bobby Bearing tells the story of a young steel ball who lives in the land of Technofear and usually avoids the dangerous Metaplanes. One day, however, Bobby is tricked by his evil cousin – he and his brothers are lured into the Metaplanes and abandoned there. Now it's up to Bobby to find his way home and rescue his brothers. The Metaplanes form a maze of isometric screens full of dead ends, junctions, and tricky mechanisms such as elevators, magnets, air ducts, and hidden switches. On top of that, enemies and traps await – evil marbles, crushing stone blocks, or perilous passages where one wrong move can send you tumbling off the screen.

meta.name: Bomb Jack
meta.desc:
En Bomb Jack, el jugador controla a Jack, quien debe recoger todas las bombas para completar la ronda actual y pasar a la siguiente. Esto suena fácil, ¿verdad? ¡Incorrecto! Numerosos enemigos como aves, momias, tortugas y orbes hacen la tarea más difícil a Jack.
nnEn cada ronda, cuando has recolectado una número de bombas saldrá una moneda de bonificación, y si recolectas esta moneda la puntuación del jugador aumentará de 2 a 4 veces. Más a menudo que no, aparecerá la "bola de poder", y cuando la recojas, puedes derrotar a los enemigos y tener más tiempo para recoger todas las bombas. También hay la moneda "E", que le da a Jack una vida adicional, pero rara vez aparece esta moneda.
nnCuanto más progresas en el juego, más difícil se vuelve, ya que los enemigos viajan más rápido para atraparte.

meta.name: Bomb Jack II
meta.desc: Tras salvar el mundo en la primera entrega, Bomb Jack ahora tiene un objetivo más… terrenal: recoger todo el oro posible, esparcido sobre repisas que alternan entre abiertas y cerradas. Las abiertas dan más puntos, y si logras recoger 10 seguidas, ¡ganarás una vida extra! Así que planifica bien tu ruta: prioriza las abiertas cuando puedas. El juego cambia respecto al original: Jack ya no se mueve con libertad. Ahora avanza a saltos (flick-style), teletransportándose solo a repisas situadas directamente en las cuatro direcciones cardinales. Y cuidado con los enemigos: sí, puedes empujarlos al vacío… pero cada empujón consume parte de tu energía limitada. Avaricia o supervivencia? Cada salto es una decisión.

meta.name: Bongo
meta.desc: Bongo sends you into a hectic gem hunt as a rat while a relentless villain stays right on your tail. On each screen, you have to deal with ladders, slides, and conveyor belts that can slow you down or speed you up. The letters of Bongo's name are especially useful, letting you teleport to different parts of the screen. Its appeal comes from fast collecting, constant dodging, and the small tricks that help you eventually rescue your girlfriend and reach the next stage.

meta.name: Bongo
meta.desc: Bongo is a single screen platform game much in a similar vein as Popeye. You control Bongo the rat as he moves about the screen attempting to collect gems while avoiding the bad guy after him. You can teleport Bongo to various parts of the screen by positioning yourself on top of the letters in his name (B O N G O) and pressing the fire button. The screens contain ladders that can be traversed up and down, slides that will slide Bongo to the bottom and conveyor belts that can impede his movement or make him move faster. Only one gem is visible at a time. After five gems have been collected, Bongo rescues his girlfriend and moves on to the next screen. Bonus letters spelling out Bongo can also be collected.

meta.name: Booty
meta.desc: Booty es un juego de plataformas. Juegas como Jim, el camarero, que aborda un barco mercante y debe encontrar la mayor cantidad de tesoros posible para obtener su botín. ¡Necesitarás elegir estratégicamente el orden en el que buscarás en las diferentes habitaciones del barco porque solo puedes llevar una llave a la vez!

meta.name: Bosszu, A - The Revenge
meta.desc: A Bosszú: The Revenge unfolds a dark tale of vengeance, drawing you step by step into a dangerous narrative. After a brutal attack, you embark on a quest for truth and retribution, guided by dialogue, choices and careful observation of your surroundings. Storytelling takes center stage, building tension through atmosphere, text and deliberate pacing, with every action carrying potential consequences – measured in flow, yet driven by constant menace and narrative weight.

meta.name: Botticelli
meta.desc: Botticelli challenges you to a battle of wits in which each side tries to guess what the other is thinking of. By asking clever yes-or-no questions, the computer attempts to uncover your chosen object – or you try to deduce its solution in return. Different categories add variety, and you can expand the system's knowledge by teaching it new objects with suitable questions. With every round the program becomes smarter – turning each session into a contest of logic, deduction and creativity.

meta.name: Boulder Dash
meta.desc: Eres Rockford, y tienes que cavar a través de cuevas infestadas de monstruos en busca de diamantes. En cada nivel debes reunir un número concreto de diamantes para abrir un portal al siguiente nivel. Los enemigos pueden ser aplastados por la caída de rocas, que quedan sueltas cuando se quita el suelo debajo de ellas o se empujan sobre un espacio vacío, pero ten cuidado porque tambien pueden aplastarte a ti. En los niveles más avanzados, la dificultad aumenta añadiendo más rompecabezas y límites de tiempo más cortos.

meta.name: Bounce!
meta.desc: Bounce! sends you along a galactic jumping route where you hop from pad to pad and must keep your head at all times. Each surface has its own effect – green pads launch you far ahead, red ones slow you down, black pads are deadly trap doors that cost you a life, and white pads offer valuable bonus rewards. Hazards await along the way, and only with precise timing and smart choices will you stay in the game – a fast-paced balancing act between risk and reward.

meta.name: Bounder
meta.desc:
En Bounder, el jugador controla una pelota de tenis que rebota constantemente por la pantalla.
El jugador debe mover la pelota entre rebote y rebote para que caiga en zonas favorables como la hierba o el suelo hexagonal principal. Si aterriza en zonas peligrosas como pinchos y arena, o si golpea a los enemigos en movimiento, el jugador pierde una de las siete vidas. Sin embargo, hay casillas de bonificación (flechas que permiten que la bola permanezca más tiempo en el aire o teletransportes que despejan una parte del juego).
Las 174 pantallas se dividen en 10 niveles. Al final de cada nivel hay una pantalla de bonificación.

meta.name: Bowling
meta.desc: Bowling is a classic top-down bowling game designed for a single player. In this simple but fun sports game, the player takes control of the bowling ball and tries to knock down as many pins as possible by making accurate throws and scoring strikes or spares. The top-down perspective allows a clear view of the lane and pins, making it easier to plan and accurately score throws. Players can adjust their throwing angle and the strength of the throw to achieve the best results.

meta.name: Break Dance
meta.desc: Break Dance (also known as Breakdance) is a Commodore 64 rhythm game that relies on players making dancing moves that resemble the classic game Simon says. A computer character performs a pattern of five break dancing moves that must be copied by the player. One new move is added to every round, making the game more complicated as the player progresses through each stage.

meta.name: Breakout Construction Kit
meta.desc: "Breakout Construction Kit" is a special version of the classic Breakout game for the Commodore 64 that offers players the opportunity to create and customize their own levels. With the construction kit approach, it brings a completely new dimension to the genre and is often referred to as "Breakout on steroids". What makes the "Breakout Construction Kit" special is the integrated level editor, which allows players to design their own obstacles, power-ups and block arrangements. This allows them to expand the classic Breakout game according to their ideas and create individual challenges. In addition to editing existing levels, completely new designs can be created, which allows endless variations and a personal gaming experience.

meta.name: Brian Clough's Football Fortunes
meta.desc:
Brian Clough's Football Fortunes is a football management computer board game suitable for two to five players. 
Each player takes on the role of a club manager. The object of the game is for each manager to successfully steer his team through the season using his skill to make his team stronger as the game proceeds. Each manager competes with human and computer controlled teams in a ten strong league and the F.A. Cup. From the second season onwards, each team will also enter a European competition (League Champions to the European Cup, F.A. Cup Winners to the Cup-Winners? Cup, and the remaining clubs will go to the UEFA Cup). 
The winner of Brian Clough's Football Fortunes is the manager who survives to the end of the game and has the highest number of game points. Game points are points awarded by the computer program, based on each managers performance in League and Cup competitions and on the amount of cash the player has left at the end of the game.

meta.name: Bubble Trouble
meta.desc: Bubble Trouble follows the classic invader formula, tasking you with stopping descending bubbles before they reach the bottom of the screen. You control a ship or character, aim carefully and shoot down the advancing formations. Every burst bubble awards points, and occasional power-ups grant temporary advantages. Despite never receiving an official release, the game earned a small but loyal following in the retro community thanks to its refined mechanics and distinctive charm.

meta.name: Buggy
meta.desc: “Buggy” gives you basic math problems where you enter two numbers to find the correct result – a test of your focus and arithmetic skills under pressure.

Cannon
meta.desc: Cannon is a straightforward yet gripping shooter where you must take down incoming enemies with precise shots. Success relies not only on accurate aiming but also on quick reflexes to dodge enemy fire and position yourself strategically. Only those who combine skill, timing, and smart movement will be able to overcome the escalating challenges and emerge victorious.

meta.name: Captain Fizz
meta.desc: The Blaster-Trons have invaded the planet Icarus, which is set as 20 levels on top of the last. The people can only be saved if a hero or two step forward and reach each of the 20 lifts in this Gauntlet-style game. Two players is the ideal, as gameplay is simultaneous and only ever fills half the screen for each player. Each level has doors which are locked with colour coded keys, as well as doors which can only be used in one direction, and lots of long corridors and mazy layouts. Both yourself and the aliens are armed with lethal lazers, although you can take several hits before dying.

meta.name: Carnage
meta.desc: “Carnage” is a top-down racing game where only winning matters – finish anything but first, and it's game over. All cars have equal speed, so victory depends on smart tactics and the clever use of power-ups. After each race, you earn £800 to spend in the shop on essentials like nitros, mines, or upgrades to gain the upper hand. With a total of 18 tracks to conquer, only those who drive aggressively and spend wisely will dominate the road.

meta.name: Castle Master
meta.desc:
Castle Master utiliza un sistema de juego en 3D en primera persona. Debes explorar un castillo medieval armado con un tirachinas / lanzapiedras para liberar a un ser querido del malvado "Magister".
El juego combina acción y resolución de puzles en cuatro torres principales de tres niveles cada una. Las puertas deben abrirse con una de las diez llaves proporcionadas. Éstas no están marcadas, al igual que las distintas pociones, por lo que resulta difícil saber qué llaves abren qué puertas y qué pociones son beneficiosas.
El contacto con ghouls o cgutes drena tu energía. Es posible recuperar algo comiendo queso. El movimiento y la puntería se controlan por separado.

meta.name: Cave Fighter
meta.desc: Cave Fighter challenges you to navigate dangerous cave systems while the screen scrolls relentlessly from left to right and time is always running out. You can move in both directions, but every jump must be carefully judged, as height and distance depend on how long the fire button is held. Safe footing is limited to floors and platform tops, while contact with walls, deadly hazards, or enemies is immediately punished. A multi-directional weapon allows you to defeat creatures, though some are tougher than others and require precise handling.

meta.name: Cave Guennie
meta.desc: Cave-Guennie is a fun boulder dash-like game. Also known as Caveman, it takes players to an underground world full of adventure and puzzles. In this game, players control Guennie, a cave character who navigates through various caves and passageways. The goal is to collect gems while overcoming dangers such as falling rocks and other obstacles. Strategic use of moves and timely dodging are crucial to overcome the challenges.

meta.name: Championship Wrestling
meta.desc: In Championship Wrestling, you step into the ring as one of eight eccentric fighters – including K.C. Colossus and Prince Vicious. With 25 moves like leg drops, spin kicks, atomic drops, and high-flying rope attacks, the game delivers pure arcade action. The more complex the move, the higher the score – you can even toss your opponent out of the ring. Timing, tactics, and showmanship determine who comes out on top.

meta.name: Chaos
meta.desc: A crosshair shooter where you have to shoot the number of aliens shown on the left before you run out of the number shown on the right. The number of incoming enemies always starts at 40. The squadrons consist of only a few elements and jump and fall as they approach. Good luck, defenders!

meta.name: Chip Factory, The
meta.desc: The Chip Factory is a single screen platform game based on the arcade game BurgerTime. You play the role of Charlie who is a trainee accountant in a Silicon Chip factory who dreams of working in the manufacturing section making the chips themselves. One night, when everyone has gone home, Charlie fires up the machines to try his hand at making the chips.

meta.name: Choplifter!
meta.desc: Choplifter es un juego de acción / arcade de desplazamiento lateral que te pone al mando de un helicóptero de ataque. Tu misión: ir tras las líneas enemigas y rescatar hasta 16 rehenes por nivel. Sin embargo, ten cuidado, porque los tanques y los aviones enemigos intentarán detenerte, ¡y no dejarán de disparar mientras rescates a esos rehenes! Originalmente lanzado para computadoras personales Apple II, Choplifter fue posteriormente portado a las salas de juegos y varias consolas de videojuegos. Seguirían muchos imitadores y homenajes.

meta.name: Circus
meta.desc: Circus starts with your car breaking down far from anywhere. Searching for a gas station, you find a circus tent in a valley, where music plays before suddenly falling silent. You approach the entrance cautiously, hoping to find fuel. The game uses an upgraded engine that accepts full sentences and allows switching between graphics and text, making the experience easier and less frustrating.

meta.name: Clowns
meta.desc: The Clowns game was released as a cartridge and is only controlled via paddles in joystick port 1. However, there is also a disk version that can be played using a joystick. The game is about using a bobbing springboard to spin two clowns upwards through the air, thereby collecting colorful balloons. The balloons you collect earn you points. The jumping clown must land exactly on the free area of the board, otherwise he will fall. After three crashes the game is over. There are two platforms each on the right and left. The jumping clown also starts there. Strictly speaking, the gameplay is taken from the game Breakout, except that instead of a ball, two clowns jump across the screen.

meta.name: Collision
meta.desc: Collision is a fast-paced lightcycle duel inspired by Tron, where your vehicle leaves a glowing trail behind. The goal is to outmaneuver your opponent, forcing them to crash into a wall or your trail while avoiding collisions yourself. Reflexes, awareness, and timing are key to survival, as the clean colors and minimalist design perfectly capture the tension of a futuristic showdown.

meta.name: Coloris
meta.desc: "Coloris" is a puzzle game for the Commodore 64. The gameplay is reminiscent of classic titles such as "Columns" or "Tetris": colored blocks fall from above and the player must arrange them so that rows or columns of the same color are created in order to score points. As you progress, the speed increases, requiring quick reactions and strategic thinking. The graphic design of "Coloris" makes effective use of the C64's 16-color palette and offers smooth animations. The soundtrack and sound effects are typical of the era and contribute to the game's atmosphere.

meta.name: Colossus Chess 4
meta.desc: Colossus Chess 4 is one of the most comprehensive chess programs available for home computers, created with modern programming techniques by an experienced chess programmer. It fully understands all official chess rules, including underpromotions, the fifty-move rule, and draws by repetition, and it can handle even difficult mating scenarios such as king, bishop, and knight versus king. In addition to the classic 2D view, it offers a realistic 3D board that can optionally be controlled with a joystick, making play clear and user-friendly. With its wide range of features, the program appeals both to casual players and to chess enthusiasts seeking the highest level of precision and depth.

meta.name: Columns
meta.desc: Columns drops you into a colorful cascade of falling gems that you control with quick decisions and sharp awareness. Three stones descend as a column, and you rotate them to line up matching colors that vanish and open space for new chains. As the stack rises, every move becomes more tense – but that mix of risk, rhythm and little bursts of luck is exactly what keeps you hooked. When chain reactions fire and the field clears in an instant, each success feels like a small rush.

meta.name: Commando
meta.desc: En Commando, encarnas a Super Joe, un soldado solitario con una M60 y seis granadas, avanzando sin cuartel por territorio enemigo. Morteros estallan, balas silban y granadas caen mientras cruzas trincheras, acantilados y lagos. Los rebeldes surgen de cuevas, búnkeres y vehículos, decididos a detenerte. No hay retirada. Solo adelante. Recoge munición, reabastece granadas y abre camino entre oleadas de enemigos hasta alcanzar la fortaleza. Cada paso es un acto de resistencia; cada disparo, una promesa de que no te rendirás. ¿Tienes la destreza, la resistencia… y los nervios de acero para llegar hasta el final?

meta.name: Convoy
meta.desc: In Convoy, you command the last gun defending the River Thames against an advancing enemy fleet trying to reach London. Your task is to destroy the ships by adjusting the elevation and bearing of your gun to hit them. If the ships manage to slip past you, they will start bombarding the city – and all will be lost.

meta.name: Cops'n'Robbers
meta.desc: You are a robber whose target is the Acme Diamond Co. and the old mine, both which are full of jewels. Once collected, the jewels need to be returned to your getaway vehicle. Making your job hard are cops who are very smart, choppers who are dangerously low to the ground, and ghosts that can kill you if you come in contact with them. You have one weapon to deal with these enemies, but it has limited ammo. If you get killed by cops or ghosts, you can enter the jail and release one of your captured men.

meta.name: Corman
meta.desc: “Corman” is a game in the style of “Jumpman” or “Lode Runner.” The goal is to collect all scattered pellets in each level while avoiding falls and dodging the flying balls that traverse the level. The game is challenging because the player character is relatively large for this type of game. Unlike “Jumpman,” the musical capabilities of the Commodore 64 are utilized, featuring continuous background music (“The Swan” from “Carnival of the Animals”) and the “Funeral March” accompanying each lost life.

meta.name: Crazy Golf
meta.desc: Crazy Golf is a fun and colorful mini golf game for the Commodore 64 that takes players into an imaginative golf world. The game is characterized by its creative and often crazy course layouts, which are peppered with various obstacles and challenges. The controls are simple and intuitive, so that both beginners and experienced players can quickly immerse themselves in the game. The different courses require strategic thinking and precise aiming to get the ball into the hole. With its charming graphic style and catchy sound, Crazy Golf offers a happy and relaxing gaming experience.

meta.name: Crazy Jumpman
meta.desc: In Crazy Jumpman, you leap through levels to collect scattered money while avoiding dangerous obstacles like falling apples and snakes. Your goal is to reach the ladder at the top of the level. Precise movements and quick reactions are crucial to overcoming the challenges and progressing. Get ready to load the next challenge after completing the first section.

meta.name: Crillion
meta.desc: Crillion is a fast-paced action puzzle game similar to Breakout, but you control the ball directly. It bounces vertically by itself, and you steer it horizontally. The goal is to clear all color-matching blocks by hitting them with the same-colored ball. Color changes are triggered by special star-marked tiles. Avoid skull blocks – touching one costs a life and resets the level. There are also unbreakable walls and slider blocks that can only be moved when the ball matches their color.

meta.name: The Crystals of Carus
meta.desc: Crystals of Carus is a single player text adventure with pictures and music. Ereth, the evil Lord Death, was jealous of the royal family of Carus for they were greatly loved by their people. He resolved to do them great harm and concocted a powerful spell which turned them all to stone. The only way to reverse the spell is to locate the five magical gems that are scattered throughout the land. No one knew how to set about this task so the royal family remained as statues for years until a traveller, the player, arrived and vowed to undo this great wrong.

meta.name: Csavargas a Gombak Birodalmaban
meta.desc: A Hungarian educational game about the different mushroom types you can find in the forest. View is in first person perspective. You collect the mushrooms to sell them on the market. (Title translated as "Stroll In The Realm Of Mushrooms")

meta.name: Cuthbert in the Tombs
meta.desc: In Cuthbert in the Tombs, you play as Cuthbert, who discovers the writings of ancient explorer Jesmon Hys and ventures into the Tombs of Ledromica in search of treasure and the hidden Temple of Rah. This flick-screen maze game challenges you to explore room by room, collecting treasures and bonuses while avoiding or shooting monsters with sideways-only fire. Monsters appear a few seconds after you enter a screen, and touching one costs a life. You'll also need keys to open doors – but you can carry only one at a time. Smart planning and quick reflexes will lead you to the hidden temple.

meta.name: Cuthbert in Space
meta.desc: In Cuthbert in Space, you're on a Federation mission to plunder the Moronians' solar system. On each planet, you must first refuel by using a remote shuttle to retrieve yellow fuel pods and return them to the mothership. As you do, you must dodge or shoot the persistent Intergalactic Police. Once fueled, you collect as much loot as possible – but when a malfunction occurs, you must quickly retrieve spare parts and take off before the Moronians' bomb detonates.

meta.name: Cyborg
meta.desc: Cyborg puts you into an escalating defense mission where your objective changes and demands new skills at every stage. You first protect a massive spacecraft from attacking enemies, using a radar for orientation and careful maneuvering to keep your damaged ship operational until a tough final foe ends the section. This is followed by a dangerous tunnel run that tests precise control as speed constantly shifts. In the final phase, you leave your ship and explore an enemy base on foot, collecting crucial items and avoiding patrols to permanently neutralize the threat.

meta.name: Cyborg Wars
meta.desc: Cyborg Wars puts you in the role of a ruler guiding the growth and direction of a nation toward inevitable conflict. You manage population groups, convert units strategically, and balance food, resources, and military power. Every decision affects offense and defense, shaping how prepared your state is for espionage, expansion, or war. The appeal lies in strategic planning, where long-term thinking matters more than quick gains.

meta.name: Dangerzone
meta.desc: Dangerzone puts you in control of a small locomotive battling waves of hostile aircraft. You collect descending parachutes to restore energy while dodging or shooting everything else. With every hit, the tension rises – only sharp reflexes will see you through the deadly zone.

meta.name: The Dark Side
meta.desc: In The Dark Side, you control a spaceship that moves up and down along the left edge of the screen. Your mission is to repel incoming waves of aliens attacking from the right. Each wave includes three attacks of the same alien type, and the enemies change color each time they're hit. They shoot back, with some shots aimed directly at you – quick reflexes and dodging are essential. You can push forward briefly, but the ship is constantly pulled back. Three hits and it's game over. Timing and spatial awareness are key to survival.

meta.name: David's Midnight Magic
meta.desc: Midnight Magic es un juego de pinball simple diseñado para 1-4 jugadores. El juego se inspira en la mesa de pinball Caballero Negro lanzada por Williams. El juego representa la primera generación de simulaciones de pinball para ordenador personal disponible con características de pinball relativamente realistas.

meta.name: Death Race
meta.desc:
Death Race is seemingly inspired by the Sylvester Stallone movie Death Race 2000 released 15 years before this game. The object of the game is to run down innocent pedestrians in your souped-up automotive grim reaper. Progress through the race courses in 8 U.S. cities, moving from west to east starting with San Diego and Phoenix. Each race consists of capturing a flag and finding the exit door, all while squishing or gunning down as many people on foot as possible while avoiding helicopters and heat seeking missiles that are actively trying to discourage this unique hobby. If the time runs out, a barrage of missiles descends on the player's car to indicate that the race is over.
The player has a certain amount of money to begin with and earns more money for completing the races alive. This money can be used to upgrade the engine, steering, tires, chassis, or weapon systems on the car.

meta.name: Decstone
meta.desc: Decstone is a clever puzzle game where you must follow a narrow path to reach the exit before the ground collapses beneath you. Each tile is marked with numbers, letters, or colors: numbers indicate how many times you can step on it before it falls, “E” marks the exit, “T” are transporters, and “I” tiles are indestructible. Colored tiles collapse all at once when you step on any of that color. Every move must be carefully planned – destroy the wrong section, and your only way out is suicide.

meta.name: Decton
meta.desc:
Decton is a clone of the C64 game Space Taxi. You fly a hovering vehicle in a closed cave, navigating from a base platform to a blue target platform, and back to the base, without touching the walls of the cave or other obstacles.
Your home platform is colored white, the other platforms start out blue, and turn yellow when visited. Only one platform at a time should be visited before you return to your home platform, mimicking a cargo pickup/delivery. Visiting more platforms at a time does not make them all go yellow, only the first counts.

meta.name: Defender of the Crown
meta.desc:
En Defender of the Crown juegas como uno de los cuatro caballeros sajones de la Inglaterra medieval, en un momento en que el país está sumido en la confusión porque el rey ha muerto y le han robado la corona. Sajones y normandos se culpan mutuamente y luchan por el control de Inglaterra.
Tras una breve introducción del propio Robin de Locksley, comienzas el juego con un único castillo y 10 soldados a tus órdenes. Luego tendrás que construir tu ejército, tomar el control de más territorios y luchar contra los tres señores normandos, pero también a veces contra tus amigos sajones.
El juego tiene varios estilos diferentes: Puedes participar en una justa en la que tienes que derribar a tu oponente del caballo con una lanza, puedes ir a saquear un castillo en busca de botín o puedes atacar otro territorio/castillo.

meta.name: Demolition Construction Set
meta.desc: Demolition is inspired by the arcade game Breakout where you control a bat to remove all the bricks on the screen over various levels. Instead of hitting a ball upwards, Demolition sees you hitting the ball across the screen from left to right, moving the bat up or down. On each screen are various blocks and these need removing with some blocks taking multiple hits to remove and some blocks that can't be removed. There are also letters and these affect your game when collected and they affect your bat, speed of the ball or just bonus points. If the ball goes past your bat then you lose one of four lives.

meta.name: Demon's Empire
meta.desc: Demon's Empire is an arcade adventure game for the Commodore 64, developed by Novotrade in 1989. In this game, the player takes on the role of a ghost hunter who fights his way through a dark, gothic world to face various demonic creatures. The game is characterized by its seething, eerie atmosphere and offers the player a challenging journey through a world ruled by demons.

meta.name: Demon's Empire

In this game, the player takes on the role of a ghost hunter who fights his way through a dark, gothic world to face various demonic creatures. The game is characterized by its seething, eerie atmosphere and offers the player a challenging journey through a world ruled by demons.

meta.name: Diabolo
meta.desc: Diabolo throws you into a fast-paced storm of sound and color as you keep the balls in play with your paddle and smash the grinning skulls above you. Each successful hit clears a bit more space while you redirect the next ball and try to stay in rhythm. The timer keeps ticking, adding pressure, but that makes every clean shot feel even better as you gain control over the chaos and push your way upward.

meta.name: Diagon
meta.desc: Diagon turns the defense of a vital energy network into a fast and intense shooter in which you have to wipe out every enemy wave on each stage. Although you can move freely around the screen, the limited firing direction forces you to pay close attention to positioning and attack timing at all times. That blend of mobility, constant pressure from multiple sides, and steady progress through new defensive levels gives the game a direct and gripping arcade appeal.

meta.name: Diamond Mine
meta.desc: In Diamond Mine, your goal is to collect as many diamonds as possible from twenty underground shafts. Each level is shown on a single screen filled with corridors and passageways. Monsters are periodically released from a central hub and can be shot, but touching one causes you to lose all collected gems. Mines are initially covered in dust, so you can only shoot through areas you've already walked. Since most diamonds are located in the corridors, careful planning is essential. The game allows you to start from one of five different screens.

meta.name: Dingbat
meta.desc: Dingbat is a side-scrolling single-screen shooter where you fly across the screen with your jetpack and fend off enemies across various levels. You can move left or right, but to travel up or down, you need to use your boosters and gravity. After destroying a certain number of enemies, an item drops, and you must collect a specific number of them to proceed to the next level. Touching an enemy or using your boosters depletes your energy, and if your energy runs out or you touch the ground, you lose one of your five lives. A destroyed enemy also drops a "B," which refills your energy.

meta.name: Dingbat
meta.desc: Dingbat is a side-scrolling single-screen shooter where you fly across the screen with your jetpack and fend off enemies across various levels. You can move left or right, but to travel up or down, you need to use your boosters and gravity. After destroying a certain number of enemies, an item drops, and you must collect a specific number of them to proceed to the next level. Touching an enemy or using your boosters depletes your energy, and if your energy runs out or you touch the ground, you lose one of your five lives. A destroyed enemy also drops a "B," which refills your energy.

meta.name: Dizasterblaster
meta.desc: "Dizasterblaster" is an action-packed shooter game in which you control a spaceship and fight waves of aliens on a single screen. The aliens appear from different areas of the screen and drop bombs that you must dodge. In addition to precise shooting and skillful dodging, you must keep an eye on your weapon's overheating and fuel supply. If the weapon is overloaded or runs out of fuel, you lose a life. Fireballs appear from time to time that refill your fuel when you touch them. After a few levels, your mothership appears, which you can dock with to receive a fuel bonus. Pay attention to the screen, avoid obstacles and use your resources cleverly to get as far as possible.

meta.name: Dizzy VI : Prince of the Yolkfolk!
meta.desc: In "Dizzy VI: Prince of the Yolkfolk", Rockwart the Troll has cast a spell on Daisy, putting her to sleep for 100 years. Dizzy sets off to save her in a compact adventure true to the series' style. You begin underground, trapped by a botched spell, but soon explore the castle in search of Daisy. Along the way, collect stars and find food to restore energy lost from hazards. If you succeed, you and Daisy ride off into the sunset – a fairytale ending to this charming little quest.

meta.name: Dizzy III : Fantasy World Dizzy
meta.desc: Dizzy's third adventure sees the introduction of the Yolkfolk, including Daisy, who has got lost in the fantasy world of the title. Dizzy sets off to rescue her, braving dragons, rats and alligators along the way. The lands are littered with coins, and as well as completing the main task you should aim to collect as many of these as possible. There are puzzles on most screens, involving collecting objects such as keys, food and water buckets, and using them on doors, flames and such. Unlike the first two game, dialogue boxes between Dizzy and various other characters are included, giving the game a sense of character and humor as well as guiding you through the puzzles somewhat.

meta.name: Dizzy Dice
meta.desc: Dizzy Dice puts you in front of a virtual fruit machine, aiming to multiply your stake or go all-in on a risky gamble. After each win, you must choose: take the money or risk it all? But that's just the beginning – after the fruit game, a dice challenge awaits, where you guess whether the next roll will be higher or lower. In normal mode, you play freely, but in bank mode, you start with $20 and try to climb through five increasingly demanding banks to reach the big payout. It's tension, risk, and thrill – all riding on the next throw.

meta.name: Domino
meta.desc: In this logic game, the focus is on strategic thinking as you race against the clock. In the center of the screen, there is a timer counting down the seconds to the potential end of the level if you don't finish in time. At the top, there is an input field, while the boxes below indicate where you need to place your game pieces. To arrange the pieces, you enter the corresponding letter of the piece and the two-digit number of the destination square (e.g., 03 instead of just 3!). If you want to use Joker or Wildcard tokens, you simply write "J" instead of the letter. To display pieces D, E, and F, press the F1, F3, and F5 keys, respectively. The game requires precise inputs and quick thinking to successfully complete the levels.

meta.name: Doomdark's Revenge
meta.desc: Doomdark's Revenge continues the saga of Lords of Midnight, weaving a far more intricate story. After Doomdark's fall, his daughter Shareth kidnaps young Morkin, son of Luxor the Moonprince, and twists his mind with dark magic. You begin with Luxor, Rothron the Wise, and Morkin's beloved Tarithel, but as the quest unfolds, more characters join to defeat Shareth and bring Morkin back. The game blends strategy, exploration, and diplomacy as you gather armies, forge alliances, and walk the fine line between power and corruption.

meta.name: Double
meta.desc: In Double, you must reconstruct two identical patterns within a set time limit. To do this, you move entire rows — either diagonally or horizontally — by one or more spaces. Each move reduces the number of remaining moves. The game ends when time runs out or when you exhaust all your moves. It features various difficulty levels and numerous stages that challenge your pattern recognition skills and strategic thinking.

meta.name: Draughts
meta.desc: Draughts presents the classic game of checkers in a straightforward and easy-to-follow way, focusing entirely on the tactical contest between two players. From its simple core rules of diagonal movement, capturing, and promotion into kings, it quickly becomes a game that demands awareness, patience, and careful planning ahead. What makes it especially appealing is the way each promoted piece opens up new possibilities, turning what first seems like a calm exchange into a much more engaging duel for control and positional advantage.

meta.name: Driller
meta.desc:
El planeta Evath tiene dos lunas, Mitral y Triscupid. Mitral ha sido brutalmente explotada por unos forajidos, los Ketar, que han huido de la luna. En el subsuelo de Mitral se han formado grandes bolsas de gas, y si Mitral explota, miles de evathianos serán aniquilados al salir Evath de su órbita y la congelación acabará con toda la población del planeta. Los científicos han descubierto que un meteorito se aproxima y chocará contra Mitral en pocas horas, una auténtica catástrofe.
Tu misión es asegurar cada uno de los 18 sectores de Mitral colocando un equipo de perforación sobre las bolsas de gas que existen en cada sector antes de que el meteorito impacte contra el planeta.

meta.name: Driver
meta.desc: „Driver“ is a racing game where the player controls a Formula 1 car, maneuvering through a multi-lane track while overtaking opponents. Speed and precise handling are crucial to progressing without crashing. Before the race, a difficulty level can be selected to adjust the challenge.

meta.name: Dudaorru Patric
meta.desc:
On Friday evening, as usual, Patric went to the restaurant on the next street and sat down with his usual table company. The word turned to the castle in the middle of the forest.
Behemot, who knew the castle, claimed that no one could get out of it. Patric, on the other hand, was convinced of the opposite. At the urging of his friends, he made a bet with Behemot that he would get out of there before dusk and touch the road sign in front of the castle. Behemot gave some advice before the adventurous journey:
"Don't fall into a hole, because you'll sink! Beware of bumps, because if you step on them, you'll fall. Also be afraid of the car, because it could come at you and hit you. The syringe is less harmful, it's only a problem if it stabs you in the back. The death head, the jaw and the missile are also dangerous, and they attack in groups. The lasers are the most annoying, they always kick in when you least expect them."
Whether Patric wins or not is up to you.

meta.name: Duotris
meta.desc: "Duotris" is a unique take on the classic Tetris game, designed for two players sharing the same playfield. Instead of competing, players must work together to complete lines and keep the board clear for as long as possible. Communication and teamwork are key to strategically placing blocks and helping each other improve their moves.

meta.name: Dynamoid
meta.desc: “Dynamoid” blends tennis and ice hockey into a fast-paced screen sport – your goal is to hit the ball into your opponent's net, either against the computer or a second player. The rectangular field sets the stage for quick action as you control the ball's direction with your paddle and can stop it with the press of a button. But stay alert – if the ball stays in your half for more than nine seconds, it's counted as a delay of game and your opponent is awarded a goal.

meta.name: Elite
meta.desc:
Pilota tu nave espacial Cobra en un fantástico viaje de descubrimientos y aventuras, una prueba suprema de tus habilidades de combate, navegación y emprendimiento.
La mayor parte del juego consiste en viajar a diferentes sistemas estelares, comerciar con sus habitantes, ganar dinero y reputación. También se puede ganar dinero mediante misiones militares, caza de recompensas, minería de asteroides, ..... Cuando el personaje del jugador gana dinero, puede mejorar sus naves con mejores armas, escudos, mayor capacidad de carga, sistema de acoplamiento automático, etc.
Independientemente de cómo gane el dinero, por las buenas o por las malas, tendrá que correr por el espacio luchando contra naves piratas y alienígenas hostiles.

meta.name: Elite Squad
meta.desc:
Once again, you're a bouncing ball, leaving destroyed pieces in your path. Clear each screen to move on to the next.
With different and beautiful level graphics and you can also jump diagonally.

meta.name: Eliza
meta.desc: In Eliza, the computer takes on the role of a therapist, asking seemingly empathetic questions based on your previous responses. It analyzes keywords and rephrases them to keep the conversation going. What feels like true understanding is just clever text manipulation. Yet Eliza managed to convince many users they were talking to a real person – paving the way for AI and early text adventures.

meta.name: Emerald Mine
meta.desc:
A conversion of the classic Boulder Dash game. The player is deep down in a cave and has to collect emeralds and diamonds while evading monsters - and the laws of gravity, which have the annoying habit of squishing him with huge rocks if he happens to stand beneath one as it falls down.
The game also has a two-player mode where two people can rid caves of their valuables at the same time - in addition to the 80 levels, there are 20 exclusive two-player levels.
