meta.name: Emerald Mine II
meta.desc: Emerald Mine II is a Boulder Dash variant and features no gameplay changes in comparison to its predecessor besides the new level editor. The player's goal is to collect diamonds which are surrounded by dirt. When walking over a dirt spot, it is cleared and a stone above it will fall down - with unhealthy results if the player or a monster is hit by it. Other items include dynamite to clear out boulders and keys for certain doors. The need to plan ahead and find out the required steps to reach the diamonds give the game a puzzle-solving character. Besides the about hundred levels in single-player, there are additional levels for two players.

meta.name: Enchanted Isle
meta.desc: In Enchanted Isle, you find yourself on the shore of an island near Australia. Ahead, you see a sign and notice obvious exits to the north, east, and south. Your next decision will determine how your adventure unfolds. What will you do?

meta.name: Enigma
meta.desc: Enigma es un juego de disparos horizontal donde controlas una nave espacial que lucha contra oleadas de enemigos. Debes derrotar a tus oponentes, evitar obstáculos y completar tu misión mientras vuelas por diferentes niveles. El juego te desafía con desafíos de dificultad creciente e intensos.

meta.name: The Saga of Erik the Viking
meta.desc: The Saga of Erik the Viking places you in the role of Erik, who returns home to find his farm abandoned and his family kidnapped. Equipped with the essentials, you embark on a rescue journey. The game blends text adventure with graphics: while the text describes each location, pictures are drawn line by line before your eyes. This slows down the gameplay somewhat, but the visuals capture the authentic feel of Viking settlements, such as a longhouse with a sunken floor, just as shown in history books.

meta.name: Escape
meta.desc: Escape places you inside a remote labyrinth cut off from civilization, a place many explorers entered but never left. Viewed from above, you navigate its winding paths in search of a hidden axe needed to break through a locked blue door on the far side. All the while, a dinosaur relentlessly hunts you, punishing every misstep. Carrying the axe allows progress but slows you down, forcing smart decisions about when to drop it and flee. Once the door is smashed, the tension escalates – a time limit begins and higher difficulty levels introduce more pursuers, demanding sharp awareness, timing, and nerve.

meta.name: European Games
meta.desc: European Games follows the classic „Games“ formula, primarily relying on joystick-waggling with some precision elements. Across five or six disciplines, depending on the platform, speed-based events require rapid left-to-right joystick movements or pressing the fire button at the right moment. Hammer throwing involves releasing the throw when the power bar is at its peak, with additional rotation control on the Commodore. Swimming demands both joystick movement and air management – surfacing slows you down. The long jump is another joystick-heavy event, requiring precise timing for takeoff and an optional jump angle selection. Rowing functions similarly to swimming but without an air supply. Weight lifting involves maintaining a high power bar level and lifting the weight before time runs out.

meta.name: Exorcist
meta.desc:
Like his father before him, Garret "Ghostfighter" is a professional exorcist, born with a special ability to see all the demons and souls occupying haunted places. On a dark night, he receives a letter from his arch-enemy Mephisto, where this mysterious character challenges and teases him with information about Margo, his long-lost love. Garret embarks on a journey to hunt down his nemesis, hoping to gather enough clues and finally discover what happened to her.
Exorcist is a hidden object game with adventure elements, where the player has to find and click on all the items listed at the bottom of the screen, on locations filled with assorted paraphernalia scattered around the scenery. List entries written in blue are hidden from view, usually concealed behind other scene features, and require some sort of interaction to become visible and available. A few of the items are stored inside the inventory, a sack at the bottom-right corner that can be clicked to show its contents. Pieces of a letter from Mephisto have to be found and reassembled to finish each chapter.
The inventory objects have to be used to solve puzzles and perform certain actions, by dragging and dropping them from the inventory to a logical portion of the scenery. The hint button is a skull at the bottom-left, that shows the position of one of the required items when activated, but takes some time to recharge after use. Mini-games appear on some locations, taking place on a separate screen where the player has to piece together the shredded Mephisto letter, reassemble a scrambled picture, set gears on a lock mechanism, restore a damaged painting, and complete other similar challenges.

meta.name: Finders Keepers
meta.desc:
The King of Isbisima has entrusted you, Magic Knight, with the task of getting a present for his daughter. He sends you into the castle of Spiteland, which is overloading with treasures to uncover. Whether you return with them to please the King, or simply flee with everything you can get, is up to you.
Either way, the gameplay is platform-based, with enemies floating around to rob you of energy on contact. There are also scrolling mazes to move through. As you collect objects (often by using some of the other items you collect, to create chemical reactions of sorts and adding an element of lateral thinking), you must try to trade them for the most valuable items you can get.

meta.name: Fire Ant
meta.desc: You control an ant that must rescue the queen ant taken hostage by a group of scorpions. She is being held in the eighth chamber, but to reach her, you must navigate a series of tunnels that are being patrolled by the scorpions, and use objects scattered throughout the chambers to proceed to the next one. These objects include getting keys that will open yellow doors, and explosives that will detonate walls. Objects placed at the proper locations usually cause a chain reaction that clears the obstacles. Occasionally, scorpions are seen laying eggs, and getting these eggs will award you with bonus points. When the queen ant is rescued, the game resets to the first chamber.

meta.name: Flash Gordon
meta.desc: From the famous comic strip comes this multi-load action game. Flash has 3 distinct tasks to face, travelling through a desolate forest in the first, shooting or avoiding minions and avoiding the holes. The top half of the screen displays the action, while the bottom half displays a map. The Cave of Barin is his target, with the aim of taking on Barin in combat using an array of punches and kicks. After this, Flash mounts a motorcycle and rides towards Ming, with guards to fend off and energy gates to pass through in order to replenish before finally taking Ming out.

meta.name: Footballer of the Year
meta.desc: Footballer of the Year puts you in full control of the career of an aspiring striker aiming to rise from the lower divisions to the very top. You manage your money, invest it in target cards, and decide when to use them to create scoring chances and potentially net multiple goals in a single match. The action sequences place you directly into key moments, dodging defenders, shooting under pressure, or taking penalty kicks. Your performances have immediate consequences – strong displays can earn you transfers to bigger clubs

meta.name: Formula 1
meta.desc: “Formula 1” is a racing game inspired by “Pitstop II.” Players engage in thrilling head-to-head races, focusing on skill, precise controls, and strategic pit stops.

meta.name: Formula 1 Simulator
meta.desc: "Formula 1 Simulator" lets you race across ten international circuits, including classics like Monza, Monaco and Silverstone as well as retired tracks such as Zandvoort and the Österreichring. You can choose between wet or dry conditions and manual or automatic gear shifting. Realistic braking and acceleration are essential to setting a competitive lap time, demanding precision and control from the player.

meta.name: Fortress Underground
meta.desc: An enemy power station has been discovered in a vast underground cave. Your mission is to destroy it. Your attack helicopter is equipped with a cannon and a limited amount of fuel. As you navigate the labyrinthine cave system, you must avoid enemy fire while heading to the next waypoint, where you can take a brief pause, save the game, and refuel your helicopter.

meta.name: Freddy Hardest in South Manhattan
meta.desc: Freddy Hardest is back – ditching space for the streets! No more starships, now it's fists and fury as Freddy takes on gangs and thugs in pure 2D beat 'em up fashion. With a limited but deadly arsenal of karate or kung fu moves, he dishes out punches, throws, and jaw-shattering jump kicks that leave no doubt who's boss. Forget laser guns – Freddy fights old-school. A single-player classic that's more bruises than bonding, and all-out retro brawling fun.

meta.name: Freddy Hardest in South Manhattan

meta.name: Frenesis
meta.desc: Frenesis is a frantic 40-level action game where you must defend a central cross extending to the screen edges against waves of aliens approaching from all angles. Your only weapon is the Statron – a line of arrows that moves horizontally or vertically across the screen. The arrows always point in the direction of movement, and only when the tip of the Statron hits an alien moving toward it is the enemy destroyed. Fast reflexes and smart positioning are key to surviving the onslaught.

meta.name: Frog Jump
meta.desc: In Frog Jump, children playfully practice basic math skills like counting and ordering. Competing against the computer or another player, they control frogs to solve tasks. The game is specifically designed for children aged 4 to 8, combining learning with fun.

meta.name: The Fury
meta.desc: In 1996, the first Rim Race took place on a specially built track called the Rim, located in the Arizona desert. The track had a diameter of 20 km and a length of 60 km, and took eight years to construct. Over 1,000 drivers participated, many of whom died, and by the final race, only Seya Alkli remained as the winner. Over the course of 45 years, the race took many forms and was broadcast throughout the entire solar system. By 2045, it reached its current form. There are 12 Rims around the planet Devs, which orbits Jupiter. You play as a driver in this dangerous sport, racing at high speeds, destroying opponents, and trying to survive. You can earn money to buy better cars and weapons. There are three types of races: Time Run, Killing Race, and Tag Race. The goal is to reach "The Fury," a mysterious point on the track where a vehicle disappears at high speed.

meta.name: Future Knight
meta.desc: In Future Knight you've crash-landed on a hostile planet and your love interest has been taken by aliens. First, you must navigate the maze-like wreckage of your ship, now swarming with alien intruders. Use your laser gun to blast them away and find the exit. Once on the surface, you face towering robot enemies – only by defeating them can you rescue the one you love.

meta.name: Future Knight

Once on the surface, you face towering robot enemies – only by defeating them can you rescue the one you love.

meta.name: Galaxians
meta.desc: "Galaxians" puts you in control of a small spaceship at the bottom of the screen, battling alien formations that descend in classic Space Invaders fashion. Occasionally, individual enemies break off and dive at you in fast, direct attacks. You'll need sharp reflexes and perfect timing to survive their increasingly aggressive assault. A retro space showdown where every shot counts.

meta.name: Galaxy
meta.desc: "Galaxy" is a Galaga-style shooter where you control a spaceship at the bottom of the screen, fending off waves of alien ships gathered in formation at the top. Enemies fire bullets and dive down in kamikaze-style attacks. Their behavior varies by color, and some require multiple hits to destroy. To complete a level, all enemies must be eliminated. With each stage, they become faster, and larger enemies use beams to restrict your movement. After several waves, a Challenge Stage appears with a new pattern and bonus points awarded based on kills. You start with three lives, earning an extra one at 20,000 points and every 70,000 thereafter. A fast-paced Galaga variant with increasing difficulty and classic arcade action.

Ghost Town
meta.desc: El antiguo pueblo fronterizo está desierto desde hace mucho tiempo, pero aún se pueden recuperar trece tesoros de la engañosa quietud de su mina cerrada y de Boot Hill. Descubre pasadizos secretos y navega por una corriente sobrenatural subyacente usando tu analizador de texto VERBO SUSTANTIVO lo mejor que puedas mientras cabalgas sobre un potro salvaje, abres una caja fuerte y aprendes las reglas del juego con el espíritu de un chamán fallecido hace mucho tiempo.

meta.name: Gold Rush
meta.desc: Gold Rush challenges you to collect gold nuggets as a prospector while avoiding obstacles such as rocks, trees, ponds, and shacks. Each level increases in difficulty with more hazards to dodge, all under a strict two-minute time limit. If you exceed the time or make too many mistakes, you lose one of your three lives. With every successful stage, the number of nuggets grows, forcing you to move faster and more precisely to keep progressing.

meta.name: Grandmaster
meta.desc: Grandmaster is a one player chess game where you play the computer at chess with ten skill levels (0-9). As well as adjusting the skill level you can also watch the computer play itself, receive hints, force the computer to play the move it is currently thinking and change board colour. You use the keyboard to type in your moves and the default setting is skill level one and you play as white.nn

meta.name: Grandmaster

meta.name: Green Beret
meta.desc: Este juego arcade te arma con un cuchillo y te envía a una misión kamikaze contra hordas de combatientes enemigos. Hay cuatro secciones de juego distintas, que incluyen una base de misiles, un puerto, un puente y un campo de prisioneros enemigos. En el camino se pueden recoger armas adicionales con munición limitada, lo que facilitará mucho la tarea. El nivel está dividido en 3 niveles horizontales, que se pueden mover saltando o usando escaleras, lo que da la oportunidad de evitar las multitudes más amenazantes de combatientes enemigos.

meta.name: Gremlins
meta.desc: En Gremlins, controlas a Billy desde una vista lateral mientras defiende su salón tras la medianoche de traviesos Mogwai y peligrosos Gremlins. Debes capturar a los Mogwai y devolverlos a su jaula, y eliminar a los Gremlins con tu espada. Cada nivel tiene un temporizador que termina al amanecer (6:00 h). Completar los objetivos a tiempo da más puntos, pero basta con sobrevivir para avanzar. Una frenética batalla contra el caos nocturno.

meta.name: Gridrunner
meta.desc: By the year 2190 the Earth is a barren and desolate wasteland. With all natural resources used up, the human race's last hopes rest with a huge orbiting solar power station know as the GRID. Now the GRID has been invaded by a race of evil droids, intent on the total subjugation of Earth. To combat these droids, an incredibly powerful and manoeuverable ship, the Gridrunner, was developed. Your mission: stop the droids and save the Earth!

meta.name: Gun Law
meta.desc: You are the only one left after a bloody alien attack and now with your weapon store emptied and destroyed you have to traverse over five levels of terrain to escape the aliens. Armed with just a gun you must shoot the enemy who are attacking you with your weapons, but you can take cover behind tree's, fences and houses. Gun Law is based on the arcade game Commando as you move vertically up each level. The game is viewed from above at an elevated angle and you use a joystick to control your soldier.

meta.name: Gunslinger
meta.desc: In Gunslinger, you play as Kip Starr, a retired Texas Ranger on a mission to save his friend from execution by the Mexicans. Stranded in the desert, your journey takes you through treacherous lands—from mine shafts and ghost towns to a final shootout with the Dalton Brothers. This graphic text adventure combines descriptive storytelling with visual scenes, allowing input via keyboard or joystick-driven menus. Every move brings you closer to your goal—or to your doom.

meta.name: Guzzler
meta.desc: You are Greedy Guzzler and your task is to move about various top view mazes shown on the whole screen to collect keys which will enable you to open a food cell and eat all the food in it. Once you have cleared a food cell you become fat and you are unable to move through thin gaps in the maze. Moving about the border of the maze is Deflator Dennis and when you touch him then you are thin again. Touch him while thin though and you lose a life. Moving about the maze are Frobbies and if you touch one then you also lose a life but you are armed with a limited number of bombs to place and kill them which can also kill yourself. After four food cells are cleared then Bonus Barry runs around the maze with a letter and if you collect all the letters you spell Guzzler and receive a bonus. Barry can also die if touched by Dennis.

meta.name: Harbour Attack
meta.desc:
Your mission is to take control of a submarine and get to the enemy port before attempting to destroy a cargo ship in three separate screen sections:
Stage one: You must move the submarine from left to right while avoiding touching mines or nets before your air supply is exhausted.
Stage two: You must destroy a certain number of planes and ships and kill or avoid an octopus. You can only shoot on the surface, thereby also increasing your air supply, which decreases underwater.
Phase three: In the final phase you are in a harbor and the view now switches to the first person perspective. The cargo ship that needs to be destroyed is at the back of the screen. You have to fire a torpedo at the screen and control it to hit a cross on the cargo ship while avoiding hitting barges.

meta.name: Harvey Headbanger
meta.desc: Rotund drinkers Harvey Headbanger and Hamish Highball are at war in this single-screen action duel with a strategic edge. Walking across each of the 64 squares will turn them to your colour. Create a 3x3 box of these squares to release cocktails (of your own colour, so only you can use them) and top up your energy. Contact costs each player energy, but the player with a higher energy level can get up first. The aim to win a point is to trap your opponent inside a box of your squares. You can play to anything from 1 to 10 points, and at 5 difficulty levels in a one-player game.

meta.name: Head over Heels
meta.desc: Headus Mouthion (Head) y Footus Underium (Heels) son dos espías del Planeta Libertad (Planet Freedom). Son enviados a Blacktooth para liberar los planetas esclavizados de Penitentiary, Safari, Book World y Egyptus, y así poder derrotar al Emperador para prevenir que otros planetas caigan bajo su poder. Capturados y separados, los espías son trasladados a la prisión del cuartel general del Castillo de Blacktooth. Primero deberán escapar. Luego, a través del mercado, deben llegar hasta la estación Lunar (Moonbase), donde podrán usar los teletransportadores para viajar a los planetas esclavizados. Allí deberán localizar y recobrar las coronas robadas. La liberación de los planetas y la derrota del Emperador permitirá a Head y Heels volver al planeta Libertad (Planet Freedom) como héroes.

meta.name: Hexaeder
meta.desc: Hexahedron takes place in a 3D maze where players navigate intricate corridors with strategic precision. The impressive 8-channel sound and the ability to customize waveforms in the "Hexa Demo" mode create a unique experience. The music is based on Johann Sebastian Bach's Suite No. 2 in B Minor (Minuet).

meta.name: Cauldron
meta.desc:
Jugando como una bruja, debes reunir los seis ingredientes necesarios para eliminar a tu archirrival, una calabaza malvada. Los ingredientes se encuentran en edificios subterráneos, cada uno de los cuales solo se puede ingresar una vez que hayas encontrado la llave adecuada. Las llaves se encuentran en la superficie, y debes volar en tu escoba para recolectarlas.
Las secciones en la superficie se juegan como los típicos juegos de disparos, ya que tu escoba no se ve afectada por la gravedad y puedes disparar balas usando tu energía mágica. Murciélagos, bolas de fuego e incluso gaviotas son algunas de las muchas criaturas que intentarán detenerte; el contacto te costará más energía que dispararles. También encontrarás recargas de energía disponibles.

meta.name: The Hobbit
meta.desc: The Hobbit is based on Tolkien's tale “The Hobbit” and puts you in the role of Bilbo, who sets out on a treasure hunt with Gandalf and Thorin. Along the way, you encounter both friendly and hostile creatures, whose partly random behavior makes each playthrough different. A unique feature is the ability to talk to characters and even animals, opening up extra interactions. The adventure is told through text with illustrations but unfolds in real time – a rare feature for interactive fiction.

meta.name: Hunter
meta.desc: Hunter has you piloting a spacecraft across a grid-like arena where you must eliminate hostile roboships that attempt to crash into you while dodging your missiles. You move between sectors, fire carefully and decide when a shot is worth taking, since missed attacks reduce your score. Clearing each screen increases enemy speed, making positioning and timing increasingly important. The experience blends accurate shooting with tactical movement in a focused action challenge that rewards efficiency.

meta.name: Hustler
meta.desc: Hustler is a 1 - 2 player pool game. The player controls the white ball by positioning a crosshair on the table. The shot power is controlled by a strength meter that rapidly fluctuates between high and low strength. Game options include 1-player any ball any pocket, balls in order, and balls in its pocket. 2-player options are score the pockets, mini pool, and lowest and highest.

meta.name: Hustler
meta.desc: Hustler is a 1 - 2 player pool game. The player controls the white ball by positioning a crosshair on the table. The shot power is controlled by a strength meter that rapidly fluctuates between high and low strength. Game options include 1-player any ball any pocket, balls in order, and balls in its pocket. 2-player options are score the pockets, mini pool, and lowest and highest.

meta.name: Icicle Works
meta.desc: Christmas is just around the corner and Santa Claus has a big problem. When he returns from vacation, he finds all of his presents scattered and buried under snow. With the potential for avalanches and all the bad-tempered penguins and polar bears running around and bothering him, it doesn't get any easier. It is the player's job to help Santa Claus and collect all the presents before Christmas.nnnnIcicle Works is a Boulder Dash variation. The player's goal in each level is to obtain a series of packages needed to build a toy. The game is time-limited and the player loses a life if they do not complete a level quickly enough.

meta.name: Ikari Warriors
meta.desc: Juegas como dos soldados, probablemente fuerzas de élite, muy parecidos a Rambo. Tu misión es "simple", tienes que cruzar las líneas enemigas, pero tus enemigos son muy numerosos. En este shooter de desplazamiento vertical, vas armado con una ametralladora y granadas, por supuesto en cantidades limitadas. A veces encontrarás vehículos como tanques con una fuerza de ataque más considerable. Atravesarás selva, ríos, zonas en ruinas, ... El juego se vuelve aún más interesante en el modo para dos jugadores (en cooperación).

meta.name: Indoor Soccer
meta.desc: Indoor football is recreated in this game notable for the large amount of colour clash during play. There are walls around the pitch, off which the ball ricochets in play, and 5 players on each team. There are no dead-ball plays other than centres and penalties. 3 skill levels are available, as are 2-player games. Off-the-ball fighting is a common feature of the game, which can lead to penalties being awarded.

meta.name: Indoor Sports
meta.desc: Indoor Sports brings together four classic indoor games: Bowling, Air Hockey, Darts, and Ping Pong. Not all games appear on every platform, but each features multiple AI difficulty levels and player-versus-player options. Bowling involves three phases — lining up, setting angle, and timing the release. Darts require aligning the shot, setting curvature, and power, with shifting views to track the dart's path. Air Hockey is fast-paced with a tilted overhead view, ending after five minutes or twelve points. Ping Pong includes a skill-balancing system to create fair matches.

meta.name: Into the Eagle's Nest
meta.desc: Into the Eagle's Nest se ambienta en la Segunda Guerra Mundial. El jugador debe infiltrarse en una fortaleza enemiga, rescatar a los soldados aliados capturados, destruir el edificio y recuperar las obras de arte robadas. La misión abarca ocho niveles, cada uno con objetivos específicos. Si bien algunas pinturas se pueden recolectar libremente, otras deben extraerse de cajas. Con munición limitada, numerosos guardias patrullando y llaves estratégicamente ubicadas, un enfoque cauteloso y calculado es clave para el éxito de la misión.

meta.name: Invaders
meta.desc: Invaders is a simple clone of the classic arcade game, where the player controls a cannon to shoot down advancing waves of aliens. The enemies move step by step closer and drop bombs that the player must dodge. The goal is to eliminate all the aliens before they reach the ground. Quick reflexes and precision are key to achieving success.

meta.name: Invaders
meta.desc: Invaders is a simple clone of the classic arcade game, where the player controls a cannon to shoot down advancing waves of aliens. The enemies move step by step closer and drop bombs that the player must dodge. The goal is to eliminate all the aliens before they reach the ground. Quick reflexes and precision are key to achieving success.

meta.name: Invincible, The
meta.desc:
In the endless, cold caves of time, he walks his way surrounded by intangible, hostile spirits, ponders his lost past and searches for his future, struggles, dies and is reborn, the Man: the Invincible.
Search the cave system for the letters of the game's title (pay attention to the correct letter order!), escape from or fight enemy spirits. You can recharge your dwindling life force with the help of some of the remaining computers of a destroyed civilization - you just have to find them in time! You can only get to the leftmost cave after completing the last letter, good luck with that!

meta.name: Jack Attack
meta.desc: Jack Attack es un desafiante juego de acción y puzles en el que debes derrotar a todos los enemigos en una cuadrícula rectangular. Puedes empujar o soltar bloques para aplastarlos, ya sea atrapándolos entre dos bloques o dejando caer uno sobre ellos. Pero ten cuidado: si un bloque o un enemigo te cae encima, pierdes una vida. Algunos niveles incluyen agua, que es mortal para ti, pero segura para los enemigos. Además de eliminar a todos los enemigos, puedes ganar puntos extra al subirte a cada plataforma, que desaparecen después de un tiempo determinado. La velocidad también se recompensa con puntos extra por completar el nivel rápidamente.

meta.name: Jackpot
meta.desc: Jackpot turns the simple idea of a slot machine into an entertaining gambling game where luck, timing, and a little bit of thought come together. You aim for the right symbol combinations, hope for bigger payouts, and try to work your way toward increasingly rewarding wins. The different odds and the relaxed pace give the whole thing a pleasantly motivating appeal. The result is a simple but genuinely engaging slot-machine adaptation that thrives on the thrill of the next big hit.

meta.name: Jetman
meta.desc: “Jetman” is an action-packed game where the player must collect fuel cubes from the bottom-left corner of the screen and deposit them on top of their spaceship. Once the ship is fully fueled, it launches, awarding the player bonus points. At the same time, the player must evade the dreaded Morf and combat the growing anti-matter columns. The laser can be used to destroy Morf and shoot holes in the columns, though both obstacles are only temporarily neutralized. Skill and strategy are essential for success.

meta.name: Jet Set Willy
meta.desc: In Jet Set Willy you guide Willy through his enormous mansion containing more than sixty interconnected rooms. Before he is allowed to go to sleep, he must collect all the scattered items around the house and tidy everything up. Each room presents its own hazards, including spikes, spinning blades and tricky jumping sections. Because many rooms have multiple entrances and are linked across different levels, the mansion quickly becomes a complex maze to explore. Careful navigation and precise timing are essential if you want to search every room and complete the task.

meta.name: Kane
meta.desc:
Un juego de acción y totalmente apasionante, en cuatro episodios, donde adoptas el papel del Sheriff en la misión de hacer la paz con los indios. Un juego para aficionarse, con música soberbia y efectos sonoros llenos de realismo
El primer nivel consiste en disparar a pájaros, con un mínimo de 3 para avanzar. Debes apuntar delante de los pájaros, para tener en cuenta la distancia que recorrerán al disparar. En el tercer nivel tienes que disparar a los malos, que emergen de los lugares (banco, taberna, tienda) en una disposición exacta del Salvaje Oeste.
En el segundo nivel se intenta saltar por encima de rocas y cactus, mientras que en el nivel 4 hay que saltar por encima de un tren a toda velocidad para hacerse con su control.

meta.name: Karate King
meta.desc: Karate King es un juego de lucha con vista lateral en una sola pantalla, del mismo estilo que The Way of the Exploding Fist, en el que debes intentar vencer al ordenador o a otro jugador. Utilizando diferentes combinaciones de joysticks, cada jugador dispone de un número variable de movimientos, incluyendo puñetazos, patadas y saltos, y por cada movimiento exitoso el jugador obtiene un punto. Cuando un jugador tiene tres puntos, ha ganado el combate y pasa al siguiente oponente. Se hace una cuenta atrás y el ganador de la pelea es el que tiene más puntos.

meta.name: Kenny Dalglish Soccer Manager
meta.desc:
En 1989, Kenny Dalglish apenas jugaba en el Liverpool y se enfocaba casi por completo en su rol como entrenador, por lo que un juego de gestión futbolística sin acción arcade bajo su licencia tenía sentido. El juego se basa exclusivamente en iconos y menús, quizás el primer paso para llevar el género a los 90. Cualquier equipo que elijas comienza desde la 4ª división. Puedes seleccionar resúmenes de los partidos, que se ven desde una perspectiva lateral y muestran goles y jugadas cercanas, aunque también puedes desactivarlos.
Comienzas con plantillas de más de 20 jugadores de las cuales eliges tu equipo de 11, cada uno con una calificación de habilidad directa. Las formaciones se seleccionan colocando a cada jugador en una posición precisa junto a la formación del equipo rival, utilizando un sistema bastante avanzado para la época. A través de un menú separado, puedes consultar con el presidente (para ver si está satisfecho con tus esfuerzos), el fisioterapeuta (para revisar lesiones), el ojeador (que sugiere jugadores para comprar) y más.

meta.name: Kikstart
meta.desc: Kikstart is a motorcycle trials racing game it allowed 2-player simultaneous (via a split-screen facility) or 1-player, vs-computer play. The basic premise of the game is to control a bike using acceleration, braking, "hopping" and "wheelies" to navigate across a course of various obstacles, from ramps and gates to telephone boxes and tires.

meta.name: Killapede
meta.desc:
Killapede is a shooter based on the arcade game Centipede where you have to shoot a centipede like creature which moves down from the top of the screen left or right before it reaches the bottom of the screen. When a part of the centipede is shot, it will split into two with both parts still advancing down the screen. Also on screen are a various amounts of mushrooms which block your movements and when the centipede hits them it will change direction. 
As well as the centipede and mushrooms to shoot there are various other creatures to shoot and these include a spider which moves in various directions at the bottom of the screen, a flea which places more mushrooms on screen, and a snail which when shot gives bonus points. If you touch these then you lose one of six lives. You can move your ship left, right, down and up the screen a certain amount of distance with a joystick or the keyboard.

Knockout
meta.desc: Knockout! is a boxing game your goal; Become world heavyweight champion. To do this you first have to become European champion. Before fights you can choose the stamina of your boxer and the computers between one and four, with one being the strongest. There is also a two-player option, which consists of just a fight between both fighters. The last option you have is the ability to create an opponent. This allows you to move the boxer around the ring, throw punches and defend yourself with a limited number of moves before you have the chance to fight him. The fights themselves are viewed from the side, with the entire ring visible on the screen and you taking control of the boxer in the red pants on the right.

meta.name: Krakout
meta.desc:
Clon de Breakout al estilo Arkanoid, que a diferencia de la mayoría de juegos similares tiene el bate en un lateral de la pantalla (hay opción izquierda/derecha) en lugar de en la parte inferior.
Hay varios tipos de ladrillos especiales, pero a diferencia de, por ejemplo, Arkanoid, no caen hacia el bate. En su lugar, cuando golpeas un ladrillo especial, éste se voltea para mostrar una letra. Por ejemplo, E (Extiende el bate), C (Atrapa la bola), M (Misil), B (Bomba, explota al ser golpeada por la bola, eliminando una serie de ladrillos) y X (Vida extra).
Los enemigos/obstáculos son más variados que en Arkanoid. Algunos hacen que el bate se congele, otros mastican y se comen la bola y te la escupen.

meta.name: Kung-fu Kid
meta.desc: The wicked Madanda has just awakened from a sleep of several thousand years, and as the hero, your job is to use your kung-fu skills to fight the thugs in each of the seven rounds before reaching him, and you have 99 seconds to complete each round. The thugs tend to attack you from behind as well as in front. As usual,  you have to defeat a boss at the end of each round in order to proceed to the next round. However, round six requires you to fight several bosses. Bonus points are awarded at the end of each round for the energy and time that still remains.

meta.name: Lancer Lords
meta.desc:
Lancer Lords is a single player combat game that has the player jousting with other knights atop an ostrich. Every victory over another knight causes the defeated ostrich to lay an egg before mount and rider disappear in a puff of smoke. These eggs must be destroyed before they hatch into another opponent.
As the game progresses the levels become harder with progressively more ostriches in the arena and shorter hatching times for the eggs.

meta.name: Las Vegas
meta.desc:
Las Vegas is a simulation of a three-reel slot machine. To increase the chances of winning, it is possible to hold certain reels (Skill Stop) or nudge them for a certain number of times if permitted; The player can also bet on an existing win. The aim of the game is to win jackpots.
Las Vegas also has a high score table and a number grid.

meta.name: Laser Zone
meta.desc: Laser Zone puts you in control of two "Zone Gunners" – one on the wall, one on the floor – using a single joystick. You must shoot incoming waves of enemies before they reach the walls, where they become untouchable and close in on you. You can move beyond screen edges, but when enemies reach your zone, things get dangerous. Use an Electric Bolt to wipe the screen in emergencies. Reflexes, awareness, and timing are key to survival.

meta.name: League Challenge
meta.desc:
An old-fashioned football management game, in which you take over one of 64 clubs and try to lead them to glory. The choice is fairly arbitrary, as you can rename the team, you always start in the lowest of the four divisions, and the kits aren't real-life.
You start with only 11 players, and the maximum is 15, so you'll be keen to sign new players, and you get this opportunity after each match, when a player is offered, with his skill and form displayed. You can train your players to improve these skills, and sell players you don't rate highly.
Matches are played out via a succession of short animations, in which the home side wears red and the away side blue. At full time you are told how many injuries were incurred. The league season features 15 matches, one against each team, with the top two teams gaining promotion.

meta.name: Leaper
meta.desc: Leaper relies on a simple and direct gameplay idea in which your goal is to collect as many red dots as possible. That clear objective creates an immediate appeal, because every movement matters and you have to guide yourself skillfully across the screen without wasting opportunities. Its strength lies in that no-frills focus on collecting, reaction, and steadily building a higher score.

meta.name: Legionaire
meta.desc: “Legionaire” puts you in the role of Caesar, commanding up to ten Roman legions against numerically superior barbarian forces in a campaign to conquer Gaul. Starting with just a few units, including cavalry and Caesar's elite legion – the strongest on the battlefield – you'll need to outmaneuver the enemy using terrain and Roman discipline. Each battle plays out in real time, requiring sharp tactics and quick decisions. Though your legions are powerful, only through clever positioning and strategic planning can you overcome the hordes. The campaign becomes increasingly difficult, making this an early and demanding real-time strategy classic.

meta.name: Legionaire

meta.name: Leonardo
meta.desc: En este juego de rompecabezas de arriba abajo, debes ayudar a Leonardo, un ladrón de poca monta, en sus movimientos criminales. Usa tu joystick para guiar a Leonardo a través de 50 niveles diferentes, incluyendo bancos, museos y almacenes. Al pulsar el botón de disparo de tu joystick, puedes empujar todo lo que esté en tu camino, excepto las paredes. Los objetos empujados no se detendrán hasta que choquen con otros objetos o con el oficial de policía y su fantasmal ayudante que intentan detener tus robos. Cada nivel es más grande que la pantalla, en la mitad inferior de la pantalla tienes un útil radar para orientarte. Si te quedas sin tiempo o te atrapa la policía, perderás una de tus cinco vidas. Si consigues dominar los diez primeros niveles, recibirás una contraseña con la que podrás empezar la siguiente partida directamente en ese nivel.

meta.name: Locomotion
meta.desc: “Locomotion” takes you back to the Wild West, where you play as a train engineer collecting gold trucks to deliver to the bank – across two game modes with varying control over speed and track switching, you couple trains, fix obstacles, evade bandits, avoid ghost trains and keep an eye on fuel and water, making every switch of the rails a choice between fortune and disaster.

meta.name: Logic (CP Verlag 1995)
meta.desc: "Logic" exige concentración y habilidad para replicar con precisión un cuadrado basado en una plantilla, todo bajo presión de tiempo. La plantilla se muestra en el lado derecho de la pantalla, mientras que el cuadrado en el que se debe trabajar está en el lado izquierdo. Usando el joystick, controlas una flecha horizontal y verticalmente entre las cuatro columnas y filas. Al presionar un botón, la fila seleccionada se mueve una posición. El objetivo es organizar el cuadrado mediante movimientos estratégicos para que coincida con la plantilla. El límite de tiempo deja poco espacio para la deliberación. Un consejo útil: Para cambiar el control de la flecha de horizontal a vertical, muévela hacia la esquina superior derecha y presiona el joystick hacia abajo. ¡Disfruta del desafío del rompecabezas!

meta.name: Logo
meta.desc: Logo offers three independent puzzle games, each with 120 levels. Between the levels, digitized images appear as a motivational factor. All puzzles must be solved within a time limit, and every five levels a password is provided. In the first game, a stone combination shown at the bottom must be replicated by placing stones with values from one to four on the playing field. Each placed stone increases the value of adjacent stones by one, with values above four resetting to one. In the second game, a given stone combination must be removed by clicking on stones. Neighboring stones also disappear, and new stones can appear in empty spaces. In the third game, stones must also be removed, but only stones of the same color and value can be taken. The values of adjacent stones increase by one.

meta.name: Mad Maze
meta.desc: In Mad Maze, your task is to find the exit from a confusing and chaotic labyrinth. A good sense of direction and patience are key to discovering the correct path through the tangled maze.

meta.name: Girl Test : Mädchen-Test
meta.desc: “Girl Test” offers a playful solution for indecisive bachelors by helping them choose between multiple women. The player inputs information about the women, and the game makes a decision, allowing the focus to shift entirely to the next steps.

meta.name: Major Blink
meta.desc: You control Major Blink over various single screens full of different size blocks and paint them. To paint a block you have to paint all the sides which will complete the whole block but there are Teddies moving around the screen removing your work. Also moving around are Drones, sent by your previous employers who weren't happy at your failure but you do have a double laser to help. The Teddies can be destroyed but the Drones can only be stunned and if you touch the Teddies or Drones then you lose one of five lives. If you stand at the top of the screen in the red zone, no more Teddies will appear and once all the blocks are painted then you move to the next screen. There are five skill levels to choose from and they are Duffer, Novice, Normal, Expert and Master.

meta.name: Maniax
meta.desc: Maniax is a Qix variant. The basic playing principle: the player controls a dot which can draw lines on the screen and when a closed figure is drawn, the color of the area changes. Additionally there are enemies, either confined to the uncolored or colored areas, which cause the player sprite to die if touched. The goal is to color 75% of the screen.

meta.name: Master Mind
meta.desc: "Master Mind" allows the player to play the well-known logic and deduction game, where a secret color combination must be deciphered through strategic guessing and reasoning.

meta.name: Mastermind (Unknown)
meta.desc: "Mastermind" allows the player to play the well-known logic and deduction game, where a secret color combination must be deciphered through strategic guessing and reasoning.

meta.name: Matrix
meta.desc: Matrix (Gridrunner 2) continues the story of Gridrunner and throws you once more into battle against an alien invasion. You take the role of a retired starship captain who returns to service because the aliens are back, stronger than ever. On the battlefield, chains of pods attack you – bizarrely shaped like flying camels. When shot, they split into smaller chains, making the fight even more intense. At the same time, cannons fire at you from the bottom and left of the screen, while a human traitor may appear and make enemy fire frighteningly accurate – though he too can be taken out. Deflectors twist your bullets or ricochet them back at you, as you advance through increasingly difficult levels where enemy attack patterns keep changing.

meta.name: Mayhem (Mr. Micro)
meta.desc: "Mayhem" is a single-screen side-view game set on platforms with lifts. As the captain of a cargo ship, you must safely transport the Blibbles, whose home planet is overpopulated, to a new planet. Chaos ensues when the Blibbles escape their holding cells, roam the ship, and enter the engine room, heading towards a cargo transporter that sends them to oblivion. Using a scanner, you control a Coerco-Stasis-Grille to transport the Blibbles to safety before they reach the transporter. Meanwhile, rogue droids appear, which can reset the Grille, and a Wafoid, which cannot be destroyed, must be avoided. The game ends when ten Blibbles reach the transporter.

meta.name: Megabolts
meta.desc:
Your task is to destroy all Megabolts who have invaded your world over 64 screens on a 8x8 grid connected by various passages. The game is a maze game viewed from above with each maze shown on the whole screen and you must control a craft killing all the Megabolt's ships that move about the maze. To kill them you can either shoot them with your laser or you can crush them with part of a wall which you fire at to move. There is also a spinning yellow craft that moves about the maze and this can't be shot just avoided. Touch this spinning craft or a Megabolt and you lose one of six lives. 
As you move about the maze you have a fuel gauge which is slowly decreasing and when this runs out you lose a live but you can collect moving orange diamonds to increase it. As well as the main playing area you can also see your score, lives, high score, how many Megabolts to destroy in that maze, your grid location and an alert which tells you which grids still have Megabolts active.

meta.name: Mercenary
meta.desc: Mercenary begins with a crash landing on the planet Targ, where you must find a way to escape. Caught in a civil war between the native Palyars and the invading Mechanoids, you can take on missions for either side, capturing resources and destroying facilities to earn money for your getaway. Multiple strategies are available, allowing you to decide whether to leave Targ simply or escape with the wealth you have amassed.

meta.name: Microchess #3
meta.desc: “Microchess #3” is a chess simulation with a minimalist design and stylized pieces – offering a strategic challenge against a computer-controlled AI. The unique representation of the pieces gives the classic game its own visual identity.

meta.name: Mindbreaker
meta.desc: Mindbreaker is a memory and puzzle game. The game challenges players to test their memory skills and logical thinking by identifying and remembering various patterns and sequences to successfully solve the puzzles. In Mindbreaker, players must remember a specific arrangement or sequence and then correctly recreate or solve it to progress. As the difficulty increases, the patterns become more complex and require more concentration and good recall, making the game a challenging exercise for the brain.

meta.name: Mine Field
meta.desc: "Mine Field" is a Pac-Man-style arcade game where the player is chased by skulls but has the ability to shoot them. Certain sections of the maze must be navigated in specified directions, adding a strategic element. Special symbols have unique effects on gameplay – some, for instance, temporarily freeze enemies.

meta.name: Mine Sweeper
meta.desc: Mine Sweeper is a variation of the classic Minesweeper puzzle game, where players must uncover safe paths without hitting a mine. Numbers on revealed tiles indicate how many mines are adjacent, requiring strategic planning. A wrong move that uncovers a mine ends the game instantly. Precision and logical thinking are essential to winning.

meta.name: Molecule Man
meta.desc: Molecule Man es un juego isométrico de laberintos. La única forma de escapar del laberinto es usar el teletransportador, pero sólo puedes usarlo después de haber recogido las dieciséis secciones de la placa de circuitos del nivel. Para reducir la dosis de radiación, también debes tomar pastillas periódicamente. Empiezas el juego con veinte de estas pastillas, pero rápidamente se te acabarán y tendrás que buscar en el laberinto máquinas expendedoras de pastillas antirradiación. Pero, por supuesto, necesitas dinero para usar estas máquinas. Así que también tienes que recoger las monedas esparcidas por los alrededores. Se pueden recoger bombas para destruir secciones de la pared y acceder a otras salas.

Para reducir la dosis de radiación, también debes tomar pastillas periódicamente. Empiezas el juego con veinte de estas pastillas, pero rápidamente se te acabarán y tendrás que buscar en el laberinto máquinas expendedoras de pastillas antirradiación. Pero, por supuesto, necesitas dinero para usar estas máquinas. Así que también tienes que recoger las monedas esparcidas por los alrededores. Se pueden recoger bombas para destruir secciones de la pared y acceder a otras salas.

meta.name: Monkey Magic
meta.desc: Based on the original television series that ran from 1978 to 1980, the player controls Monkey as he makes his way through four levels and rescue the princess from the evil dragon. In the first level, the player rides a cloud, and must use lightning bolts to destroy three waves of flying creatures. In the second level, the player must collect all the fruit while avoiding or jumping over creatures. Vines and ladders must be navigated in order to get some fruit. The third level has the player navigating a steep cliff. Four birds drop rocks onto the path, causing it to crack. The player must jump over these cracks. Finally, in the last level, the player must defeat the dragon by throwing spears at it, while avoiding its fire.

meta.name: Monty on the Run
meta.desc: “Monty On The Run” continúa la historia de Monty, el topo, quien debe huir del país tras escapar de prisión, antes de que sea demasiado tarde. Este juego de plataformas repleto de puzles te reta a guiar a Monty a través de peligrosas pantallas llenas de enemigos, trampas aplastantes y caminos complicados con cuerdas y plataformas. No todas las zonas son accesibles a la vez, y recolectar dinero y objetos útiles es esencial. Antes de empezar el juego, debes elegir cinco objetos de un conjunto de 21 para crear tu "Kit de Libertad". Solo la combinación correcta te permitirá escapar, lo que requiere un poco de ensayo y error.

meta.name: Moon Buggy!
meta.desc: Moon Buggy puts you in command of the ATMB vehicle on a moon base under alien attack. Your mission: stop the enemy incursion. You speed up, slow down, jump over craters, blast rocks, and fend off saucers, rockets, tanks, and landmines. The buggy only moves forward, but with rockets and skill you earn points for destroyed enemies and distance traveled. The game records the top five scores of the session.

meta.name: Motor Mania
meta.desc:
Motor Mania es un juego de conducción con vista superior en el que la pantalla se desplaza verticalmente hacia arriba mientras intentas conducir tu coche de carreras durante tantos kilómetros como puedas. Comenzando en una gasolinera, puedes controlar tu velocidad hasta 80 mph, frenar y moverte hacia izquierda o derecha mientras evitas a otros conductores y diversos obstáculos. Hay tres tipos de carreteras para conducir (autopista, carretera secundaria y camino de tierra) y debes evitar golpear los laterales y a los otros conductores o perderás una de tus cinco vidas. 
Los obstáculos que encuentras afectan diferentes aspectos del coche, como baches que hacen que su dirección sea errática, parches en el camino que lo frenan, avalanchas y cruces con vehículos que se mueven a travesando el camino. Las gasolineras aparecen de vez en cuando y aquí podrás repostar, arreglar el coche y recargar de la batería.

meta.name: Mr. Puniverse
meta.desc: In a land of dinosaurs and hardships, humans have to be tough to survive but unfortunately a new breed of human has started appearing 'The Wimp'. Unable to hunt, these men need a special vitamin to keep them alive but these are in dangerous places with many hazards. You play one wimp, Mr. Puniverse as you attempt to collect vitamins in this flick-screen platform game. One or two players take it in turns after a loss of life trying not to let your energy bar decrease fully or you lose one of eight lives and you lose a life if you fall from a height. There are objects that fire at you and pillars that move up and down, and if these are touched then a life is also lost.

meta.name: Nailboard
meta.desc: Nailboard is a simple yet strategic two-player skill game that hinges on timing and precision. Each player has five counters and must choose a row in which to place each one. Pressing the RETURN key determines how far the counter travels – the longer you hold it, the farther it goes. The goal is to land your counters in high-scoring zones, earning more points than your opponent. However, players can't choose the same row twice in a row, so smart planning is just as important as accurate timing.

meta.name: The Nectar of the Gods
meta.desc: You begin your adventure in a hotel room on the second floor of modern Athens, with a breathtaking view of the ruins of the Acropolis. Your mission is to find the Nectar of the Gods, a magical wine that grants immortality. On your journey, you will travel through various locations in Athens and possibly venture into the surrounding mountain paths and ancient sites. Along the way, you will solve puzzles, gather clues, and uncover secrets that bring you closer to your goal. But be on guard, as the search for the nectar may involve dangerous trials and unexpected encounters with mythical creatures or rival adventurers.

meta.name: Nibbly '92
meta.desc: Nibbly '92 is a snake game where you guide a constantly growing serpent through a maze. The goal is to eat all the dots on the screen without trapping yourself. Once a maze is cleared, a new one appears. After sixteen levels the mazes repeat, but with higher difficulty as the snake grows faster, eventually making the puzzles nearly impossible to solve.

meta.name: Ninja Master
meta.desc:
Ninja Master combina cuatro pequeños juegos secundarios en uno.
Eres un aprendiz de ninja. Quieres impresionar a tus compañeros y convertirte en un auténtico ninja. Es la hora de los juegos anuales, y tienes que competir y triunfar en ellos para lograr tu objetivo. Los juegos constan de cuatro eventos:
Evento 1: el evento de la flecha
Evento 2: Romper un bloque de madera
Evento 3: La prueba de la estrella Shuriken
Evento 4: La prueba del dardo
En todas las pruebas, hay una puntuación de clasificación y tienes tres intentos para superarla. Si lo consigues, pasas a la siguiente ronda. Si fallas, vuelves al principio.

meta.name: Number Builder
meta.desc: Number Builder is a math learning game where you play as a builder moving around a construction site, climbing ladders, and collecting sums to reach a target number. By adding or subtracting from the starting value, you must solve three goal numbers per level before the bucket is raised. Twelve difficulty levels, different characters with varying speeds, and a self-test mode provide variety.

meta.name: Number Chaser
meta.desc: Number Chaser is a math learning game in the form of a car race, where the goal is to solve math problems as quickly as possible. You steer your car across four lanes, choosing the one with the correct answer. Wrong answers briefly stop the race, while correct ones bring the next problem immediately. Oil slicks, rocks, and night driving add extra challenges before your final score is shown.

meta.name: Numbers
meta.desc: Numbers puts you up against the ball, gravity, and your own reflexes in a classic pinball showdown. Score points by hitting targets, triggering bonuses, and keeping the ball in play as long as possible. The pace increases with each hit, and one slip means the ball drops out of play. Simple rules, clear goals – but only sharp reflexes will earn you a shot at the high score.

meta.name: Oblido
meta.desc:
Oblido is an arcade puzzle game where you have to move four sets of nine counters made up of different colours and patterns into the same coloured reception area over various levels. The game is viewed from above with the whole level shown on the screen as a maze and each level has to be completed within a time limit using a cursor. The maze is made up of two areas which are coloured blue and grey and you can move anywhere through blue areas but are unable to enter grey areas so you must use the pathways to move to different parts of the level.
The counters are scattered all over each level and can only be pushed through blue areas but you are able to push as many counters as you like in a line. As you are pushing counters, sinners move about the pathways from a sin bin and if you touch one then you lose time and it is game over when the time runs out. You have the ability to fire at the sinners to kill them but they can also fire back if you are anywhere on the level which loses more time when shot.

meta.name: Olympic Skier
meta.desc: Olympic Skier sends you onto the slopes to score as many points as possible across several winter sports challenges. Slalom, ski jumping, and downhill each demand a different approach, so you constantly have to adapt to new movements and risks. It is not only about finishing, but about the performance that is rated afterward. That mix of varied events, score chasing, and sporting competition gives the game its entertaining appeal.

meta.name: One Man and his Droid
meta.desc: Control a droid on the planet Andromadous trying to herd Ramboids (alien sheep) into a teleporter. First the droid has to travel through a chamber filled with constantly moving Ramboids. Then the Ramboids must be herded towards the teleport receptor in a particular order. As well as the droid's standard movement mode, it can also tunnel or dig through the caverns. In later levels, the cavern designs become more complex and moving blocks create further problems.

meta.name: One-on-One
meta.desc: One-on-One brings basketball legends like Julius Erving and Larry Bird straight to the virtual court and sets a new standard with smooth animation and detailed gameplay. From fall-away jumpers and steals to blocks and spin moves, each action feels like a real duel, complete with personal fouls and a 24-second shot clock. The highlight is a backboard-shattering dunk that summons a grumpy janitor to clean up the mess, muttering censored complaints. With customizable score goals and even instant replay, this game is both a competitive showdown and a humorous milestone in sports gaming.

meta.name: Othello
meta.desc: Othello is the digital version of the well-known board game played on an 8x8 grid. Both players begin with two pieces placed diagonally in the center. The goal is to have the majority of pieces in your color by the end of the game. To capture, you place a piece so that a line of your opponent's pieces is trapped between your new piece and another of your own, whether horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. Those pieces are then flipped to your color. Players take turns until the board is full or no further moves can be made. Strategy, foresight, and sharp observation determine the winner.

meta.name: Othello

To capture, you place a piece so that a line of your opponent's pieces is trapped between your new piece and another of your own, whether horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. meta.name: Overload
meta.desc: Overload is a challenging puzzle game where you strategically select and place numbered tiles on a grid. Each number shows how much pressure the field can take before it overloads, triggering an explosion that affects adjacent spaces. With every move the tension rises, as you decide between careful tactics or daring chain reactions that can reshape the entire board. The goal is to outsmart your opponent and be the last one in control.

meta.name: Overload

meta.name: P.O.D. : Proof of Destruction
meta.desc: In P.O.D.: Proof of Destruction you pilot a small spaceship across a grid that represents your civilization's power network. Movement is restricted to the four main directions along the grid lines while aliens fire relentlessly from every angle – no spot is truly safe. Their shots temporarily cut sections of the grid, limiting your movement until the lines reform. Every fourth stage switches things up with a bonus round focused purely on blasting enemies for extra points, though danger still lurks. Across 25 challenging levels, you must survive until the timer runs out. You begin with three lives, earning one extra per completed level (up to five), but time never resets after losing a life.

meta.name: Pacmania
meta.desc:
Not to be confused with Pac-Mania by Namco, this is a Pac-Man clone where the object is to eat dots and power pills. Several ghosts appear in their hideout at the center of the maze, and these ghosts will eventually make their way through the maze, chasing Pac-Man wherever he goes. However, eating the power pills cause the ghosts to turn blue, allowing Pac-Man to hunt a ghost down and then eat it for bonus points, which are also awarded for eating a fruit that appears somewhere within the maze. Warp tunnels are located at either side of the screen, and these can be used to get away from ghosts that are on Pac-Man's tail.
Besides power pills, also scattered around the maze are white H icons. If Pac-Man comes in contact with any of these icons, he will be teleported to anywhere in the maze. Once Pac-Man eats all the dots and power pills in the maze, he will advance to the next one, whose structure differs from the previous.

meta.name: Pac-Man
meta.desc:
Uno de los juegos más populares e influyentes de la década de 1980, Pac-Man está protagonizado por un pequeño comepuntos amarillo que se abre camino para despejar un laberinto de varios puntos y frutas que habitan en el tablero.
El objetivo de Pac-Man es desafiado continuamente por cuatro fantasmas: el tímido fantasma azul Bashful (Inky), el fantasma rojo que se arrastra Shadow (Blinky), el veloz fantasma rosa Speedy (Pinky) y el olvidadizo fantasma naranja Pokey (Clyde). Un toque de cualquiera de estos fantasmas significa una pérdida de vida para Pac-Man.

meta.name: Pac-Mania
meta.desc: Pac-Mania es una variación del juego Pac-Man. Debes guiar a Pac-Man por un laberinto y comerte todos los puntos del tablero para pasar a la siguiente ronda. Numerosos fantasmas multicolores también deambulan por el laberinto tratando de detenerte. Si te comes una de las bolas de energía del laberinto, los fantasmas se volverán azules temporalmente y huirán de ti. Pac-man puede ganar puntos de bonificación al comerse los fantasmas cuando están en este estado. El laberinto ahora está en 3-D y es más grande que la pantalla que se desplazará para seguir la acción. Para ayudar a salir de apuros, Pac-Man ahora tiene la capacidad de saltar. ¡Pero ten cuidado, porque algunos de los fantasmas también han aprendido este truco y podrías terminar en una colisión en el aire!

meta.name: Pancho
meta.desc: Pancho hops across platforms in an isometric view, coloring tiles just by landing on them. A wild Muncher chases him, costing a life on contact. A bouncing bean resets tiles but can be caught. Pancho starts each level on one of two flying carpets that offer safety – the Muncher can't reach them. Pressing fire teleports him to a carpet, but only four times per level.

meta.name: Petch
meta.desc:
Based on the arcade game Pengo, Petch has you playing as a penguin who must survive various mazes made of ice for two minutes to move on to the next maze. The whole of each maze is shown on the screen as you try to avoid other creatures and also try to get as many bonus points as possible within the two minutes. 
Bonus points are awarded for lining up diamonds in a vertical or horizontal line, and also for killing four or more creatures within one minute. To kill any creatures you can push blocks of ice towards them to crush them, but if you touch one you lose one of four lives and a new maze layout appears. Blocks of ice can also be removed by pushing against them if they are next to another block or the edge of the maze.

meta.name: Paperboy
meta.desc: Basado en el juego arcade, el objetivo de Paperboy es repartir periódicos a tus clientes mientras causas el mayor daño posible a las casas de quienes no son clientes. Para complicar aún más las cosas, te encontrarás con numerosos obstáculos, como obreros de la construcción, neumáticos descontrolados, patinadores, perros y gatos, coches e incluso algún que otro tornado.

meta.name: Paratroopers
meta.desc: Paratroopers is a fast-paced single-player game where you control a ground-based gun and try to shoot down enemy paratroopers and aircraft. Every helicopter, bomber or parachutist you destroy earns you points – but every missed shot costs you. If too many soldiers land, they form a tank and destroy your gun. You can shoot parachutists directly, target their helicopters or hit their parachutes for a rather brutal landing.

You can shoot parachutists directly, target their helicopters or hit their parachutes for a rather brutal landing.

meta.name: Pengo
meta.desc: In Pengo you play a penguin trying to survive ice mazes for two minutes to progress. You avoid enemies, crush them with sliding ice blocks, and score bonus points by aligning diamonds or defeating four or more enemies within a minute. Touching an enemy costs a life and triggers a new maze layout. Ice blocks can also be removed by pushing them against walls or other blocks.

meta.name: Petals Of Doom
meta.desc:
You are on a strange alien world trapped in a network of caverns which are connected by energy curtains and they can only be opened by making sure all plants in the cavern are fully grown. Viewed from the side as the screen scrolls left and right when you move, Petals of Doom has you having to blast the creatures that dwell in the caverns, making sure they don't stamp on the plants. Before you play the game you decide how many lives you require from one to six.
As you protect each plant, they will grow over time, and when all have grown you can move to the next cavern but will shrink when touched by an alien. The growth of the plants is indicated by a bar and when it empties then the plants have fully grown. Another bar to watch is your power bar and this decreases if you touch an alien, fire your laser or fly your jet-pack and when empty you lose a life but will increase if you aren't touched or don't use your laser or jet-pack. When tall enough, the plants can be stood behind and the aliens can't touch you. A radar at the top of the screen shows the location of any aliens and the keyboard or joystick is used to control your hero.

meta.name: Pengo
meta.desc: In Pengo you play a penguin trying to survive ice mazes for two minutes to progress. You avoid enemies, crush them with sliding ice blocks, and score bonus points by aligning diamonds or defeating four or more enemies within a minute. Touching an enemy costs a life and triggers a new maze layout. Ice blocks can also be removed by pushing them against walls or other blocks.

meta.name: Petchess 4000
meta.desc: Petchess 4000 is a chess simulation with a minimalist design and stylized pieces – offering a strategic challenge against a computer-controlled AI. The unique representation of the pieces gives the classic game its own visual identity.

meta.name: Petchess 4000
meta.desc: Petchess 4000 is a chess simulation with a minimalist design and stylized pieces – offering a strategic challenge against a computer-controlled AI. The unique representation of the pieces gives the classic game its own visual identity.

meta.name: Phantom
meta.desc: “Phantom” is a skill-based game in the style of “Skramble,” but without any shooting – the focus is entirely on dodging. The player pilots a jet through a hazardous landscape filled with vertically launched missiles, which must be avoided with quick reflexes and precise movements. Progressing safely earns points, but a single mistake can lead to disaster. With no weapons to rely on, survival is all about maneuvering – a game that puts reflexes and concentration to the test.

meta.name: Phantom
meta.desc: “Phantom” is a top-down maze action game where you play as a professor tasked with cleansing four cursed buildings of ghosts, monsters, and hellish portals. Armed with a laser, you fight through four floors per building, keeping an eye on energy and fuel while collecting keys, bombs, and other power-ups. The goal is to clear each floor before moving on to the next – a fast-paced battle for survival against the supernatural.

meta.name: Pheenix
meta.desc: Pheenix is a clone of the arcade shooter Phoenix: In five different screens, you have to shoot various flying entities whose droppings prove harmful for your spaceship. Luckily, you can activate a shield that will protect your vessel for a short time (you can't shoot during that time either though).

meta.name: Pin Point
meta.desc: Pin Point is based on the game Spindizzy where you control a spinning top over ten flick-screen isometric levels to reach a white flag. Each level is a route of platforms and you must guide the top without falling of the edges within a time limit. If you fall of the edge then you start at the beginning of the level wasting valuable time. You must be aware of the inertia of the top as it will keep moving after moving downhill so the joystick will need pressing in the opposite direction. To get up some hills or clear gaps you can give a little burst of speed by pressing the fire button.

meta.name: Pipe Mania
meta.desc: En este rompecabezas abstracto, cada nivel te reta a armar una red de tuberías para que el flooz —una sustancia misteriosa— fluya por la mayor extensión posible. Las piezas aparecen al azar: hay siete tipos: tramos rectos (horizontal/vertical), curvas en las cuatro direcciones y cruces que permiten flujo simultáneo en ambos ejes. Todas admiten entrada por cualquier lado. Cuando el flooz encuentra un extremo abierto o una pieza incompatible con la anterior, la tubería se cierra y se puntúa según su longitud. Planificación rápida, adaptación constante y decisiones bajo presión: cada conexión cuenta, y un giro mal colocado puede cortar el flujo… y tus esperanzas. Elegante, adictivo y sorprendentemente profundo.

meta.name: Play Your Cards Right
meta.desc: Play Your Cards Right is a computer version of the British game show, first shown on ITV in 1980 and hosted by Bruce Forsyth. The TV show featured two couples playing against each other, but this version is for either one (against the computer) or two players. The game was divided into two parts: a question and then high/low playing with cards. A player is asked the question of how many out of 100 people gave a particular answer. The other player would then say whether the actual answer would be higher or lower. If the second player was wrong, the other player played the cards first and vice versa.

meta.name: Pod
meta.desc: In Pod you guide the small hero on his quest to retrieve the stolen Magic Orb. A dragon has raided the village and taken the artifact once brought by Pod's grandfather – now it's up to you to save the village. The game scrolls sideways as you move through forests, collect diamonds, and avoid deadly obstacles. But Pod can't walk – he moves only by jumping. By alternating the up and down controls, you charge the jump gauge, allowing longer leaps the more it's filled. Along the way, birds and club-wielding giants threaten to drain your magic bar, and when it's empty, you lose one of your three lives. Touching fences or water, however, means instant death.

meta.name: Poker
meta.desc: Poker is a poker game where you compete against computer opponents. It uses classic 5-card poker rules. After the deal, betting begins, with raises doubling the previous bet. Players may discard up to three cards to improve their hand before a second betting round. Then the hands are revealed. The goal is to win all your opponents' money – or lose all of yours.

meta.name: Powerball
meta.desc: “Powerball” is a Breakout clone with classic gameplay where the player uses a paddle to destroy blocks by skillfully placing and moving a ball. The challenge lies in keeping the ball in play while clearing all obstacles.

meta.name: Powerball
meta.desc: “Powerball” is a Breakout clone with classic gameplay where the player uses a paddle to destroy blocks by skillfully placing and moving a ball. The challenge lies in keeping the ball in play while clearing all obstacles.

meta.name: Powerball
meta.desc: “Powerball” is a Breakout clone with classic gameplay where the player uses a paddle to destroy blocks by skillfully placing and moving a ball. The challenge lies in keeping the ball in play while clearing all obstacles.

meta.name: Prisoner
meta.desc: Prisoner challenges you to escape from a heavily guarded prison. Seen from a top-down perspective, you explore different areas such as the cell block, laundry, or cafeteria while avoiding the patrols of the guards. One careless move can end your escape attempt instantly. Depending on the chosen difficulty level, timing becomes trickier, and only with patience, skill, and a solid plan can you slip past the guards and make it to freedom.

meta.name: Project Nova
meta.desc:
Project Nova is a 1st person shooter where you are the pilot of a space craft and you must explore various sectors to find alien ships and destroy them. Before you play the game you can select which level you want to start on (1-8). You start the game looking out into empty space inside the cockpit of your craft with the option to look out from the rear. On your instrument panel which is shown on the top and bottom of the screen is your energy status, speed, your rating, shield status, view and how many hostiles in the area. Pressing 0-9 on the keyboard controls your speed but uses up energy so must be used carefully. Pressing F1, F2 or F3 activates your shield from Green (No Shield), Amber (20% capacity) and Red (Full) which also requires the use of energy.
Pressing C brings up your ships computer which gives a status report on various parts of your craft and a map of the area containing all the sectors. Each sector contains a number telling you how many hostiles are in that area and also which ones contain energy to top up your energy supply. These sectors can still contain alien ships. You move a cursor onto the sector you require to explore and press H for hyperspace. Once you are in the sector you use the joystick to control your craft to move a laser sight and pressing fire to fire a laser. You are also able to repair your ship if you receive damage but receive to much and it is game over.

meta.name: Prospector Pete
meta.desc:
Prospector Pete is a game heavily influenced by the game O'Riley's Mine where you control Prospector Pete over 16 mines trying to collect gold, as well as gas and oil. The game is viewed from the side with the majority of the level shown on the screen with the screen scrolling to the left or right. You must collect all the gold on each level before a ladder appears at the top of the screen to exit the mine.
As you start tunneling around the level, a stick of dynamite will blow and rising water will fill a tunnel and flow around all tunnels you have made, touch the water and you lose one of three lives. Also meanies will roam the tunnels and you lose a life as well if touched. To help you against the meanies you have a limited number of dynamite which can be dropped to stop them.

meta.name: Psychedelia
meta.desc:
I think this had been trying to come into existence for a while; I'd had odd thoughts about dynamic interactive pattern generators before, but never actually sat down to write any code. Then one day I was out running and an just appeared unbidden in my head. I still remember the exact bit of road I was on when it happened }:-). It was a simple algorithm, just seeding patterns along a path; the patterns were to expand and change shape and colour over time. I got back from my run and coded up the algo - it fit in about 1K of 6502 assembler code. I ran the code and a white cursor appeared on the screen. i picked up the joystick, moved the cursor, and pressed down the fire button.
In that instant my life changed }:-).

meta.name: Pulsar
meta.desc: Rejuvenator is a straightforward yet gripping shooter where you must take down incoming enemies with precise shots. Success relies not only on accurate aiming but also on quick reflexes to dodge enemy fire and position yourself strategically. Only those who combine skill, timing, and smart movement will be able to overcome the escalating challenges and emerge victorious.

meta.name: Punchy
meta.desc: Como su nombre indica, Punchy se desarrolla en un teatro. Es una adaptación de "Punch & Judy": encarnas al jorobado Bobby y tu objetivo es abrirte paso a través de 16 escenas teatrales para rescatar a la bella Judy de las garras de Guiñol. Cada escenario está lleno de los peligros típicos de una obra de teatro tradicional, como cocodrilos, pasteles de nata y tomates podridos, así como fosos que tienes que cruzar utilizando plataformas móviles. De vez en cuando tendrás la suerte de atrapar una salchicha que vuela por los aires mientras te mueves.

meta.name: Punchy
meta.desc: Como su nombre indica, Punchy se desarrolla en un teatro. Es una adaptación de "Punch & Judy": encarnas al jorobado Bobby y tu objetivo es abrirte paso a través de 16 escenas teatrales para rescatar a la bella Judy de las garras de Guiñol.
