meta.name: Purple Turtles
meta.desc: Purple Turtles challenges you to cross a river by using moving turtles as a living bridge while collecting fruit from the far side. You plan each step carefully, wait for safe positions, and move at the right moment as turtles suddenly dive and reappear. One mistake costs progress, making timing and patience essential. As difficulty increases, multiple turtles move at once, raising the tension – a skill challenge that rewards awareness, rhythm, and precise decision-making.

meta.name: Purple Turtles
meta.desc: Purple Turtles challenges you to cross a river by using moving turtles as a living bridge while collecting fruit from the far side. You plan each step carefully, wait for safe positions, and move at the right moment as turtles suddenly dive and reappear. One mistake costs progress, making timing and patience essential. As difficulty increases, multiple turtles move at once, raising the tension – a skill challenge that rewards awareness, rhythm, and precise decision-making.

meta.name: Puzzle (Maciek Kozicki)
meta.desc: "Puzzle" is a classic sliding puzzle game where players must rearrange a scrambled image or number grid by strategically shifting tiles into the correct order. The game requires strategic thinking and forward planning, as tiles can only be moved within the designated play area. The challenge lies in solving the puzzle as quickly as possible while reconstructing the correct positions of the individual pieces.

meta.name: Puzzle Shuffle
meta.desc: Puzzle Shuffle challenges you to restore a scrambled picture under time pressure. Once the game begins, the individual pieces are mixed up, and you have to move them back into place with as few moves as possible. On the left, you keep track of time, points, and the high score, while every move needs careful thought – a direct puzzle game for anyone who likes to spot patterns, combine quickly, and improve their result.

meta.name: Puzzlenoid
meta.desc: In Puzzlenoid, you're presented with two grids of golden blocks – the left shows the starting pattern, the right the target. Your task is to manipulate the left grid to match the right one, all within a limited number of moves and time. Each action must be carefully considered, as points, time, and moves are restricted. A game for calm thinkers with a sense for structure and logic.

meta.name: Puzznic
meta.desc:
El reto en cada nivel de Puzznic consiste en maniobrar una selección de bloques hasta colocarlos en su posición para despejarlos todos, haciendo contacto con bloques del mismo estilo. Controlas una cruz direccional que puede mover un bloque a izquierda o derecha, haciendo que caiga si no hay ningún bloque debajo del espacio que ocuparía.
Resolver los niveles presenta una serie de complicaciones. Las plataformas móviles hacen que tengas que mover un bloque en un momento determinado, o en un orden concreto, para evitar que se bloqueen.

meta.name: Pyramide
meta.desc: „Pyramide“ challenges you with a tricky puzzle inspired by the „Towers of Hanoi“ concept. Playing as Fridolin McSandwühler, you must relocate a pyramid threatened by a construction tycoon to a safe place. Your goal is to move the stones from the left to the right side of the platform while maintaining their original order – all while following strict rules. You can only pick up the top stone and place it either on the ground or on two other structures. Every move is counted, and a high-score list records your best results. Patience, intelligence, and strategic thinking are key to successfully rebuilding the pyramid and securing your spot in the leaderboard!

meta.name: Quickdraw
meta.desc: „Quickdraw“ puts the player to the test in a deadly duel against six gunmen — only the faster one survives. Each opponent is a master marksman, but so is the player — the only thing that matters is who fires first. The stakes are high — drawing too early is punished. The game demands total focus and perfect reaction time, as the enemy will draw at a random moment between two and five seconds after appearing. Only those with nerves of steel will walk away from this showdown as the winner.

meta.name: Raster Runner
meta.desc: Set in the future, the object of Raster Runner is to guide your bike around the arena while trying to surround your opponent with the energy trail your bike leaves behind. Your opponent is doing the same. When one of the bikes crash, the player who took control a bike loses one life. A crash can be avoided by activating a force field, but only up to three can be activated. The player who loses all of their lives loses the game, while the other player wins. To assist you, a small overview map can be used to keep track of the bikes.

meta.name: Reach For The Sky
meta.desc: Reach for the Sky is a shoot 'em up in the vain of Capcom's arcade classic 1942. You fly over the Pacific Ocean during the WWII and take down waves of enemy planes while avoiding their counter-fire. Weapon upgrades are earned if specific formations of enemy waves (different to each level) are taken down with no escaping planes. At the end of each level you have to battle with a giant bomber.

meta.name: Reach For The Sky
meta.desc: Reach for the Sky is a shoot 'em up in the vain of Capcom's arcade classic 1942. You fly over the Pacific Ocean during the WWII and take down waves of enemy planes while avoiding their counter-fire. Weapon upgrades are earned if specific formations of enemy waves (different to each level) are taken down with no escaping planes. At the end of each level you have to battle with a giant bomber.

meta.name: Renaissance
meta.desc: “Renaissance” es una versión digital del clásico juego de mesa Othello, cuyo objetivo es terminar la partida con más fichas de tu color que las del oponente. Al colocar tu ficha vertical, horizontal o diagonalmente frente a otra, volteas todas las fichas del oponente a tu color. Los jugadores alternan turnos hasta que no les queden más movimientos. Esta versión ofrece diversas funciones: seleccionar el nivel de habilidad del ordenador (1-9), intercambiar colores, deshacer movimientos, probar contraataques y cambiar la situación del tablero. Un cursor controlado por joystick te permite colocar las fichas en el tablero.

meta.name: Rescue From Zylon
meta.desc:
Deep underground on the planet Zylon, 28 geologists are trapped and with the water levels rising quickly, you have been tasked to fly your airship into the caverns and rescue them all. The game is flick-screen with each cavern shown on the whole screen viewed from the side with a passage connecting other caverns. You control the airship with your joystick and when you move the airship it keeps moving until you move the joystick in the opposite direction to slow it down and even stop.
Hitting any part of the cavern and walls gives you a damage point and when you collect ten then it is game over but rescue a geologist and it is reduced. As you explore and fly through the caverns there is a clock counting down showing the water level and as the clock gets lower, caverns will flood killing the men. If you touch the water then it is game over. Once you collect all 28 geologists and return to the surface you then attempt the next level which is the same layout but now introduces acid droplets which increase your damage. There are eight levels in total and each one introduces more acid and makes them fall faster.
As well as the main playing area you can also see your score, damage, ship-depth, hi-score, bonus for geologists rescued and the water depth. Before you play the game you can select which level to play and the control sensitivity of the airship.

meta.name: Revs
meta.desc: Revs is a racing simulation that puts you into the world of Formula 3 and is known for its realistic approach. Unlike many racing games of its era, it allows you to adjust aerodynamic settings and race on a fully three-dimensional track. You can even drive in the wrong direction or leave the track entirely. The game is considered a precursor to the developer's later Formula 1 simulations, already showcasing an early focus on authenticity and attention to detail.

meta.name: Road Warrior
meta.desc:
Every year a vicious biker gang has a race. You are fed up with this gang taking over so in your souped up car you become the Road Warrior. You must drive along the road shooting the bikers down. Remember to keep an eye on your fuel. If you don't kill a certain amount of bikers, when you stop for fuel you are killed.
The game is viewed from above your vehicle as you control your speed and steering with your joystick. Fire button fires your cannons. The screen shows apart from your vehicle and road, your speed, fuel, score and how many bikers to kill.

meta.name: Robin of Sherwood : The Touchstones of Rhiannon
meta.desc: Robin of Sherwood: The Touchstones of Rhiannon es un juego de aventuras que te permite encarnar al famoso Robin Hood. Está prisionero, así que debes escapar para empezar. Un personaje te llama durante tu huida y te pide que vayas en busca de las piedras mágicas de Rhiannon para cumplir la profecía. ¡Ten cuidado de no ser atrapado por tus carceleros durante tu búsqueda!

meta.name: Rockman
meta.desc: “Rockman” introduces new levels in the style of “Boulder Dash.” Players must once again apply clever strategies to overcome obstacles, collect diamonds, and avoid deadly dangers. The new challenges require precise timing and foresight to successfully complete the levels.

meta.name: Rockman
meta.desc: “Rockman” introduces new levels in the style of “Boulder Dash.” Players must once again apply clever strategies to overcome obstacles, collect diamonds, and avoid deadly dangers. The new challenges require precise timing and foresight to successfully complete the levels.

meta.name: Rogue Trooper
meta.desc: Rogue Trooper takes place on the devastated planet Nu-Earth, where an endless war rages between the Norts and the Southers. During a crucial mission, the Souther forces are betrayed and massacred in the Quartz Zone, with only one genetically engineered infantryman surviving. With the help of the biochips of his fallen comrades Helm, Gunnar, and Bagman, he sets out to prove the betrayal and bring the traitor to justice. To do so, he must recover eight scattered videotapes that hold the evidence. While taking down enemy patrols, he must also avoid sustaining too many hits in order to complete his mission.

meta.name: Room Ten
meta.desc: Room Ten takes place in the zero-gravity arenas of the Galcorp worlds, where boredom is cured with a new form of extreme sport. It's essentially a three-dimensional Pong duel – a fast-paced test of reflexes, timing, and precision. The goal is to shoot the ball toward your opponent so they can't return it. If the ball hits the opponent's back wall, you score five points, and the first to reach 35 wins. The screen displays both players' viewpoints simultaneously for added intensity. Speed, AI difficulty, and color scheme can all be adjusted – and with the two-player mode, the futuristic training chamber quickly becomes a real battleground.

meta.name: Runner
meta.desc: In Runner you pilot a spaceship over hostile terrain, battling through multiple waves of enemies. You must shoot down enemy ships, avoid obstacles, and keep an eye on your shield and fuel levels. Each wave introduces new enemy types – a classic survival challenge with fast scrolling and increasing intensity.

meta.name: Runner
meta.desc: In Runner you pilot a spaceship over hostile terrain, battling through multiple waves of enemies. You must shoot down enemy ships, avoid obstacles, and keep an eye on your shield and fuel levels. Each wave introduces new enemy types – a classic survival challenge with fast scrolling and increasing intensity.

meta.name: Saboteur
meta.desc: Eres un mercenario altamente entrenado en las artes marciales. Te emplean para infiltrarte en un edificio central de seguridad que está camuflado como almacén. Debes robar un disco que contiene los nombres de todos los líderes rebeldes, antes de que la información sea enviada a todas las estaciones remotas. Trabajas contra reloj, tanto en robar el disco como en escapar. Debes entrar en el edificio por el mar en bote de goma; luego sólo podrás salir por medio del helicóptero que encontrarás en el tejado. Tendrás que encontrar tu camino por el almacén, usando las propias municiones de los guardas en tu lucha.

meta.name: Saboteur
meta.desc: Eres un mercenario altamente entrenado en las artes marciales. Te emplean para infiltrarte en un edificio central de seguridad que está camuflado como almacén. meta.name: Saloon Shootout
meta.desc: Saloon Shootout puts you in the boots of a trigger-happy gunslinger keeping order in a dusty Western saloon – with your revolver and lightning-fast reflexes. Bottles, cards, and mice dart across the screen, and you must shoot them quickly before they vanish. Each hit scores points, but missed shots or bad aim will cost you time and score. It's a wild and comical Western shootout that tests your skill, speed, and steady hand.

meta.name: Sanxion
meta.desc: Sanxion te pone al control de un avión futurista, luchando a través de interminables oleadas de enemigos coloridos. El juego se destaca por su vista dividida: una perspectiva panorámica a vista de pájaro en la parte superior y el campo de batalla principal debajo. La velocidad de desplazamiento está determinada por la posición de tu avión: más lento cerca del borde izquierdo, más rápido hacia el centro. No hay jefes finales ni potenciadores, pero después de cada etapa entras en rondas de bonificación donde el desafío es disparar, recolectar o esquivar objetos en una carrera contra el tiempo.

meta.name: Adventure #10 - Savage Island Part One
meta.desc:
Savage Island es el único juego de varias partes de la serie de aventuras de Scott Adams, todas ellas con un parser y una interfaz muy similares. Al final de este juego, los jugadores reciben una contraseña que les permite jugar la segunda parte. Los juegos de Savage Island son también probablemente las aventuras más difíciles de la serie.
El jugador comienza atrapado en una pequeña isla en el océano, que se enfrenta a un huracán y también tiene un volcán activo. El jugador puede cruzar un río y también tendrá que explorar una red de cuevas. Los objetos se suelen encontrar recorriendo la arena con las manos.
La isla guarda un gran secreto, que sólo se descubrirá cuando se terminen las dos partes del juego.

meta.name: Adventure #03 - Secret Mission
meta.desc: Esta es la tercera aventura de la serie de aventuras de texto de Scott Adam. Se desarrolla en una central nuclear. Debes correr contrarreloj para cumplir tu misión o el reactor podría estar condenado. Usas comandos de una o dos palabras para moverte y manipular objetos.

meta.name: Sensitive
meta.desc:
Sensitive is an action puzzle game with a very simple premise. The player controls a ball which has to roll over a set of platforms to the exit. The trick is that each tile disappears after the player has rolled over it. This requires careful planning of which path to take across the platforms to get tot the goal.
Each level is a single screen with grey and yellow tiles. Rolling over the yellow tiles will cause them to disappear, the grey tiles won't dissolve when the ball rolls over them. Later levels introduce special tiles, like tiles you can roll over twice, one-way tiles and teleporters
There are no level passwords so each new game starts from the very beginning with a fixed set of 4 lives.

meta.name: Skramble
meta.desc: „Skramble“ is a typical example of the many clones of the arcade classic “Scramble” – even the name is among the most common for such copies. You pilot your spaceship through a cave system divided into six sections. Pressing the joystick button fires both your front-mounted gun and bombs simultaneously. Missiles will launch into your path to intercept you – you must shoot them down and bomb every fuel tank you see, or you'll run out of fuel. The second cave introduces aliens moving vertically in your flight path. In the third section, you'll face fireballs that are immune to your bullets.

meta.name: Falcon Patrol
meta.desc: Falcon Patrol is a two-way side-scrolling shooter. Players fly a jet across a looping area and must shoot down attacking aircraft. A small radar at the bottom of the screen shows the enemies' position. Fuel and rockets are limited, but there are several landing platforms where the jet can be refueled and resupplied. Before the jet is able to land however, it needs to be brought to a full halt right above the platform, leaving it vulnerable to attacks. Landing platforms can also be destroyed if they are bombed or enemy planes crash into them. As the game progresses, enemies attack in increasingly large and difficult waves.

meta.name: Smash
meta.desc: "Smash" is an unusual tennis game played from a 2D side view. This means players cannot move sideways but can move forward, backward, and jump, similar to the "Super Volleyball" games. When hitting the ball, the controls allow adjusting shot height and power but not trajectory due to the viewing angle. The camera always follows the ball, so players are not always visible, but a mini-map keeps track of their positions and the ball. There are four male and four female players, each with unique stats and nationalities. The game offers a choice between a single match or a knockout tournament. The number of sets and games per set can be customized in single matches but progressively increase as the player advances through the tournament.

meta.name: Snooker
meta.desc: Snooker & Pool is a double pack that includes the games Snooker and 8-Ball Pool. The game is shown from a top-down perspective, allowing you to see the entire table. After the white ball is placed in the semi-circle, you can select the desired direction for the shot by positioning a cursor. Optionally, you can also choose an effect for the shot from eight different directions, and the strength of the shot is determined by moving a power meter. The game can be played against the computer (on three difficulty levels: Beginner, Amateur, and Pro) or against another player. Additionally, the game includes an editor to set up trick shots.

meta.name: Snowball
meta.desc: Snowball places you aboard a vast starship that has finally reached its destination system, as you awaken in darkness with little sense of where you are. Through text commands you explore the futuristic vessel, examine rooms, collect items and uncover the purpose behind the mission. Gradually you piece together the layout of the enormous craft and reveal connections that extend far beyond your starting point. The experience thrives on atmosphere, exploration and the slow unfolding of a larger science fiction narrative.

meta.name: Soccer Boss
meta.desc:
Take control of a football team in a division below the top one, and try to lead them to glory in both league and European competition. You start with a squad of 12 players, whose names can be changed, but have room for 15 players in total, with chances to buy and sell players. You can take out a bank loan, the level of which depends on your division.
The team's formation can be customised, and as outfield players are not limited to a single position, you can customise the team's strengths and weaknesses in quite a precise way. Matches are played out by listing scorers as they happen, and are followed by a full list of the division's other results. Finances change after each match to to wages, gate receipts, and possible incidents such as riots and illegal player approaches.

meta.name: Soccer Director
meta.desc: Soccer Director puts you in charge of the business side rather than the touchline. Your ultimate goal is to gain majority control of the country's top football clubs by acquiring shares. You buy and sell stock, take out loans, call shareholder meetings, and even place bets to increase your funds. At the end of a season, you must hold at least 501 shares in the top ten clubs to succeed. Strategy, careful planning, and timing are key to building your football empire.

meta.name: Soko-Ban
meta.desc: Soko-Ban is a classic puzzle game, the basis of numerous clones in the later years. It is set in a warehouse. On each level, the player must push crates (from square to square) to get them onto designated spots; once each crate is on a marked spot, the level is complete. Crates can only be pushed one at a time (so two crates next to each other cannot be pushed together), and cannot be pulled--so it's possible to get a crate stuck in a corner, where it cannot be retrieved! By the last levels, you must plan 40 steps in advance.

meta.name: Solitaire
meta.desc: In Solitaire sortierst du Karten verschiedener Werte und Farben, um sie in der richtigen Reihenfolge abzulegen. Jede Spalte kann Karten aufnehmen, die genau aufeinander folgen, wodurch du taktisch planen und Züge vorausdenken musst. Mit jedem Spielzug wächst die Herausforderung, die perfekte Ordnung zu schaffen, ohne dich in eine Sackgasse zu manövrieren. Ein klassisches Kartenspiel in minimalistischer Form, das Geduld, Übersicht und strategisches Denken belohnt.

meta.name: Space Fiends
meta.desc: In "Space Fiends," you take control of a spaceship to protect a research base from aliens abducting geologists during their work. You move along the bottom of the screen, firing upward to destroy waves of attacking aliens dropping bombs. To rescue scientists, you must shoot the aliens holding them captive. Additional aliens appear on the surface, moving across the screen. If you touch them or are hit by a bomb, you lose one of your six lives. Your mission is to safeguard the scientists and eliminate all the aliens.

meta.name: Space Mission
meta.desc: “Space Mission” is a text-based survival adventure where the player must fight to stay alive after crashing on Mars. With only 24 hours of oxygen and limited resources – a ray gun with four shots, binoculars, a pint of water, and four hand grenades – the goal is to reach the supply base 200 miles away. The environment offers different paths, but sand dunes, hills, and mountains create obstacles. Every decision matters to complete the mission and survive.

meta.name: Space Warrior
meta.desc: In Space Warrior you pilot a spacecraft from a cockpit-style 3D perspective while battling enemy ships in space. As you fly through the starfield, hostile craft appear on your radar and in front of you. Your goal is to lock onto them with your targeting system and destroy them before they can damage your shields. Each enemy destroyed increases your score, but hits will reduce your shield strength. Quick reactions and accurate aiming are essential to survive and achieve a high score.

meta.name: Space Maze
meta.desc: “Space Maze” puts the player in the role of a spaceship pilot testing a new craft. The objective is to navigate through a maze without hitting the walls while avoiding passing asteroids. The spaceship changes color to signal changes in the maze's size. At the end of the flight, the player is ranked based on performance and precision.

meta.name: Space Ship
meta.desc: In Space Ship you find yourself on an alien planet where robots have stolen your spacecraft. To escape, you must search through several rooms of a base to locate your ship. Hostile robots roam the area and will electrify you if they manage to capture you. You must avoid them and carefully explore the rooms until you find your spacecraft. If you succeed, you can escape and warn Earth about the robots' planned attack.

meta.name: Spectipede
meta.desc: In Spectipede you face increasingly hectic waves of enemies and try to survive as long as possible. The main creature moves unpredictably across the field and splits into multiple segments when hit, making things more chaotic. Additional threats appear that alter the playfield and force you to adapt constantly. Each enemy destroyed earns points, but the pace keeps increasing — only quick reactions and strong awareness will keep you alive and help you achieve a high score.

meta.name: Speed King
meta.desc: Speed King es un juego de carreras de motociclismo en el que compites o entrenas en diez circuitos famosos, como Silverstone, Monza o Daytona. Antes de la carrera, eliges el número de vueltas y tu nivel de habilidad (principiante a profesional). La carrera, en perspectiva en tercera persona, te enfrenta a 19 rivales. La moto se controla con joystick, y además de regular la velocidad, debes cambiar manualmente entre seis marchas. Al terminar, se registran tus tiempos por vuelta y puedes seleccionar un nuevo circuito.

meta.name: Speed King

La moto se controla con joystick, y además de regular la velocidad, debes cambiar manualmente entre seis marchas. Al terminar, se registran tus tiempos por vuelta y puedes seleccionar un nuevo circuito.

meta.name: Spiky Harold
meta.desc: In Spiky Harold you have limited time to prepare for winter – as a hungry hedgehog you explore multiple areas, trying to eat as much as possible. You move from screen to screen, avoid dangers, and collect food before time runs out. You must stay alert, as enemies are everywhere and cost you lives on contact. Pickups can help or hinder you, making every choice important. A fast-paced race against the clock where reflexes and awareness are key.

meta.name: Spore
meta.desc:
Spore's location is a laboratory where dangerous spores are cultivated. Unfortunately, an outbreak occurred and the spores will soon infect places outside the laboratory. The only way to stop them is to use every available antidote against them - but the canisters are in said lab...
So the player's task is to travel through the non-scrolling levels and collect all the canisters. He can use his handgun to kill the spores that attack him - but they are replaced by a destructible spore generator. Extras that can be collected in the levels are helpful: flasks regenerate life energy and pills can be activated at any time to drive away spores for a short time. Other special fields include earth (which can be destroyed by the player or the spores being passed through) and impenetrable barriers (which can be deactivated and are sometimes reflected by mirrors).

meta.name: Spy vs Spy
meta.desc:
El cómic homónimo de la revista cobra vida en este juego diseñado para uno o dos jugadores. La innovadora pantalla dividida muestra a los dos espías al mismo tiempo. Juega con un amigo o contra la máquina.
El Cuervo Negro y el Cuervo Blanco están listos para luchar antes de que suene la campana. Encuentra los artículos necesarios buscando en los cajones, armarios y muebles de la casa. Burla a tu oponente colocando trampas creativas en diferentes habitaciones (una bomba en un cajón de la cómoda, por ejemplo).
La lucha se produce cuando ambos espías entran en la misma habitación. Encuentra armas ocultas en las habitaciones para ayudarte en estos combates, ya que uno de vosotros morirá si os cruzáis.

meta.name: Squirm
meta.desc: In Squirm you take on the role of a hungry creature navigating a maze to collect eggs laid by the Queen – but you are not alone. The guarding worms constantly chase you, and any contact costs you one of your lives. As you try to gather enough eggs, you must also collect glowing worms, because after a short time the lights go out and you'll be moving almost blindly. Once you collect enough, a special phase begins where you must avoid colliding with your own trail. Quick reactions, good timing, and sharp awareness determine whether you succeed or get lost in the chaos of the maze.

meta.name: Star Trader
meta.desc: Star Trader takes place in a distant star system with eight planets, once thriving through trade until pirates began raiding the routes. Traders had to choose between fighting and dying or paying a quarter of their cargo in taxes – most gave up or fled. You are the last remaining trader, now tasked with moving goods between all eight planets, turning profits while staying alert to the ever-present threat of pirates.

meta.name: Star Wars
meta.desc: Star Wars brings the arcade classic to the C64, letting you relive the film's most iconic moments. Across three stages that repeat with increasing difficulty, you first battle in space, then fight your way across the Death Star's surface, and finally attempt the legendary trench run. The goal is to endure as long as possible, because once your shield energy is depleted, the game is over.

meta.name: Starforce Nova
meta.desc: Starforce Nova takes you to a bleak future where Earth has been devastated by wars, alien invasions, and resource depletion. As Captain Paul Squires, you escort a carrier ship on a desperate search for a new home through the mysterious Dimension F. Flying your fighter, you defend the carrier sector by sector from alien attacks, offering humanity a final hope for survival.

meta.name: Stellar 7
meta.desc: Gir Draxon, Supreme Overlord of the Arcturan Empire has declared war on the Terran Federation. The leaders of Earth respond by sending the Terran Fleet on their way to Arcturus to intercept Draxon's armada. But this was a deadly mistake: the Arcturans have discovered warplinks, which allow them to transport troops from one link to another instantaneously, even across vast interstellar distances. With the fleet halfway across the galaxy, the enemy has already reached the Sol system and occupied Saturn's moon Titan. Now, humanity's only hope is the experimental Raven armored vehicle. One courageous pilot must take the Raven through seven different star systems, from Titan to Arcturus, defeating any and all Arcturans, using the warplinks to travel from one system to the next.

meta.name: Steve Davis Snooker
meta.desc: Steve Davis Snooker brings the rules of English snooker to the screen and can be played solo or with two players, but without a computer opponent. You score not only for potting balls correctly but also concede penalty points for fouls or misses, which are all tracked. After every shot you remain at the table, even if you foul or miss, making solo play more aggressive than real snooker since you never leave an opponent an opening. You can play with either 10 or the full 15 reds. After potting a red you select one of the six colors, chosen by its point value key. For each shot you set the aim, the shot power, and the spin.

meta.name: Stone Age
meta.desc: Stone Age is a tricky puzzle game where you guide a dinosaur through caves to reach the exit. You can only step on specific blocks: some are ordinary, some can be pushed in the direction of an arrow, some crumble after use, and some contain colored keys needed to unlock matching locks. Two difficulty modes determine how much time you get per level, and running out of time costs a life. Levels are coded, so you can continue later without losing progress, though your score resets.

meta.name: Storm
meta.desc: Storm throws you into a fast-paced adventure where you move through equally sized rooms to rescue Storm's kidnapped wife from a heavily guarded laboratory. Together with a wizard companion, you battle waves of enemies, collect treasure, and gradually take control of each area. The experience relies on quick decisions, constant movement, and smart enemy handling – only those who stay alert and push forward consistently will reach their goal.

meta.name: Storm

Together with a wizard companion, you battle waves of enemies, collect treasure, and gradually take control of each area. The experience relies on quick decisions, constant movement, and smart enemy handling – only those who stay alert and push forward consistently will reach their goal.

meta.name: Street Olympics
meta.desc:
Street Olympics is a one player game that allows you to participate in four different events trying to beat the records. There are two running events, one running and jumping, and a throwing event and you can play them all one after the other or practise each one. 
The first two running events are 200m sprint and 800m jogging. You waggle the joystick (or keyboard keys) left and right as fast as you can. There is a distance bar that lets you know how far left to run. There is a beep and the clock stops when you have completed the run but you have to stop waggling to stop or you will just keep running.
The running and jumping event has you waggling the joystick to run but when a obstacle appears then you press the fire button to jump. The throwing event has you running waggling the joystick and when you reach the line you hold the fire button down and a angle chart appears. Let go of the fire button at the best angle and you throw the object.
Each event is viewed from the side as your athlete moves from left to right with the screen scrolling as you move.

meta.name: Strip Poker
meta.desc: Strip Poker has you playing five-card draw not just for chips, but for clothing – or rather, the gradual removal of it from your digitized opponent. Win hands, and models like Suzi or Melissa will reveal more, all in chunky pixel graphics. Expansion disks offered new faces (and bodies) for the curious. Poker mechanics are basic, but the game's appeal was less about strategy and more about risqué novelty – a product of its time, more infamous than refined.

meta.name: Sudoku
meta.desc: In Sudoku, you fill in a 9x9 grid so that each row, column and 3x3 square contains all the digits from 1 to 9, with no repetitions. The game offers nine difficulty levels, with the number of missing digits varying - from a few gaps on the easiest level to many on the most difficult. Your goal is to solve the puzzle with as few errors as possible and secure your best time on the high score list.

meta.name: Sun Star
meta.desc: Sun Star puts you in command of a special spacecraft tasked with collecting energy from 16 solar grids. In each level, viewed from a first-person perspective with radar support, you must gather ten energy crystals. Crystals appear when one of four disruptors is shot, but vanish quickly – timing is key. Shooting and crashing drain your ship's energy, which can only be restored via hyper warp. But each warp costs you one collected crystal and can only be used a limited number of times per level.

meta.name: Super Ball
meta.desc: Super Ball relies on a simple but demanding arcade concept in which you move through a confined area while avoiding dangerous obstacles. Its stripped-down presentation keeps the focus entirely on reflexes, timing, and accurately judging movement, so even small mistakes are punished immediately. That clear and direct design is where the game draws its appeal, creating a pleasantly tense sense of pressure with very little.

meta.name: Super Cobra
meta.desc:
Super Cobra es un juego de disparos de desplazamiento lateral basado en el juego de arcade.Eres un piloto de helicópteros en una misión de vigilancia en territorio enemigo, tu objetivo es superar las diversas carreras de obstáculos y obtener tantos puntos como sea posible.
Los edificios altos, las montañas, los túneles estrechos y varios enemigos (como tanques, cohetes, minas y más) pueden interponerse en tu camino y destruir su helicóptero si no tienes cuidado.
Para ayudar a defenderte, el helicóptero está armado con una ametralladora y bombas que pueden usarse para destruir los tanques y cohetes enemigos.
Para hacer la tarea más difícil, tu helicóptero tiene una cantidad limitada de combustible.
A lo largo de los paisajes hay tanques de combustible : si uno de estos se dispara o bombardea, se le otorgará combustible extra.

meta.name: Supermaze
meta.desc: In Supermaze, you are trapped in the center of an invisible maze. Your goal is to find the exit as quickly as possible. If you want to see the maze, you can press the joystick's fire button — but it will cost you 10 seconds. Speed and a good memory are essential!

meta.name: Super Pipeline
meta.desc: Super Pipeline es un juego de puzle y acción cuyo objetivo es mantener despejadas unas tuberías para que fluya el agua. Los enemigos incluyen saboteadores que taponan las tuberías, bichos que caen del techo y, en niveles avanzados, un monstruo que patrulla la tubería. El jugador dispone de una pistola para eliminar bichos y saboteadores, y al monstruo (solo por detrás). Además, le sigue un ayudante que retira tapones y mata bichos pequeños. En fases posteriores, muros protegen a los enemigos mientras suben por una escalera derecha hacia el tejado. Cada nivel termina al acumular una cantidad determinada de agua en el barril final.

meta.name: SuperSprite
meta.desc: SuperSprite turns the controlled fall of a tiny hero into an unusual dexterity challenge in which you have to guide him safely through several movable barriers. Instead of relying on traditional movement in every direction, the game centers on shifting gaps into the right position, making each round feel like a short and tense exercise in timing and anticipation. That oddball concept gives it a distinctive appeal, because even small mistakes can block the way and every successful descent has to be earned.

meta.name: The Survivors
meta.desc: The Survivors tasks you with rescuing large numbers of people from a damaged dome complex by coordinating several specialized droids. Each has a distinct role – one digs tunnels, another teleports survivors to safety and a third moves heavy obstacles, forcing you to approach each situation strategically. As you progress through the levels, falling rocks and unstable terrain create constant danger, making mistakes costly and planning essential. The combination of unique abilities and a fragile environment creates a tactical rescue experience driven by timing, order and awareness.

meta.name: Sword Of Destiny
meta.desc:
You are Kalok and you fought so hard and so brave but in the end the evil warlock, Xorphas finally defeated you and killed you. Problem is instead of your soul going to warrior heaven, Xorphas has sought revenge by stealing your heart which means your soul is destined to wander for ever in Limbo. You still have the Sword of Destiny so you decide that you will descend into the depths of the Warlock's lair, The Abyss of Death and get your heart back. You must fight off evil nasties as well as find flashing objects which open up more of the Abyss. Scattered around are small volcano type mounds and when you jump on them they allow you to jump up higher. Touch any nasty or fall from a height then your Astral power bar will decrease slightly. When it is empty it is game over. 
Sword of Destiny is a one player flick-screen platform game. You use a joystick or the keyboard to control your hero.

meta.name: Tapper
meta.desc:
Tapper es un juego de acción donde eres un camarero y tienes que servir cerveza a los clientes más exigentes. Hay cuatro clientes, cada uno tiene su propia barra más larga que la anterior, a los que hay que mantener a raya. Si llegan al final de la barra sin recibir su cerveza, estás perdido, si se le pasa una y se derrama accidentalmente se pierde una vida y si uno de ellos te devuelve la jarra y no puedes cogerla, se perderá una vida también.
De vez en cuando aparecerá un item extraño en una de las líneas; si lo tomas, invocará un espectáculo estilo "cabarette" que mantendrá ocupados a algunos de los clientes.

meta.name: Taskforce
meta.desc: Snide Gaintree and his Mercenaries has taken over a top-secret Air-Force base in the South Pacific containing cruise-missiles, and in less than four hours will be in control of the missiles. You have been sent in a state-of-the-art jetfighter to fly through the various caverns under the base to stop Gaintree in this side view flick screen shooter. As you fly you will encounter various enemy as well as gun-placements and lasers used as gates and if you touch any of these then you lose a life (five on the C64, nine on the C16). Your plane is armed though with various weapons from a standard forward shot to limited 3 direction, vertical shot and a laser. You also have limited shields but more can be collected by shooting barrels. Each level is 20 screens and at the end is an end of level boss that needs destroying to move to the next level.

meta.name: Tazz
meta.desc: Tazz sends you through a series of dangerous rooms as the yellow Tazzman, where every bug has to be eliminated with your laser gun. Since you can only fire in four directions while standing still, every movement becomes a small risk, especially with enemies chasing you and the walls slowly closing in. Bombs give you valuable breathing room until the last bug is gone, the exits open, and the next room awaits. Bonus stages pleasantly break up the tension and reward careful collecting.

meta.name: Match Point
meta.desc: Match Point brings tennis to the screen with full rules and a pleasantly lively presentation. You fight your way through quarter-finals, semi-finals, or the final, play longer matches, and have to use ball speed, direction, and spin cleverly to outplay your opponent. Small details such as spectators following the action, ball boys, and the shadow beneath the ball add extra atmosphere to each rally. The result is a polished tennis game that effectively combines sporting precision, tournament flair, and exciting exchanges.

meta.name: Terra Cognita
meta.desc:
Los ingenieros mineros del planeta Krion han desencadenado sin querer su destrucción y deben huir en este shoot 'em up con scroll vertical. Hay más de 100 pantallas por atravesar, con barreras, pasos estrechos y campos de fuerza. También hay zonas de salto temporal: algunas te adelantan, otras te devuelven al inicio.
Los enemigos son escasos —el juego recuerda a Artificial Dreams, del mismo creador—. La nave tiene combustible limitado, que se rellena con fichas. Otros potenciadores dan puntos extra, vidas adicionales, modifican la velocidad de scroll o conceden invencibilidad frente a campos de fuerza (no contra enemigos).

meta.name: Tetris
meta.desc: En el Tetris se juega con los tetrominós (duatro elementos), son el fundamento para las combinaciones que se deberan de realizar, capa a capa, tratando de no dejar vacíos; cada capa completada se eliminará, otorgando puntos al jugador, así sucesivamente, nivel a nivel, aumentando progresivamente la velocidad de caída en los tetraminós.Jugar al Tetris de forma moderada (media hora al día por un período de tres meses) incrementa las funciones cognitivas tales como pensamiento crítico, razonamiento, procesamiento del lenguaje​.

meta.name: Thai Boxing
meta.desc: In Thai Boxing you face a single opponent in direct combat and try to defeat him with well-timed attacks. Viewed from an angled perspective, you control your fighter using different joystick movements to perform high, middle or low strikes. At the same time you must dodge or block incoming attacks to avoid taking too much damage. Successful moves earn you points, with more difficult or effective attacks giving higher scores. If neither fighter is knocked out, the one with the highest score after three rounds wins the match.

meta.name: Thrust
meta.desc:
Thrust introdujo una mecánica única en los arcades: física realista y movimiento basado en inercia, influyendo directamente en títulos como Oids y en Exile del mismo autor. La acción, en vista lateral y gráficos vectoriales, refuerza su naturaleza matemática.
Como miembro de la Resistencia, debes penetrar en líneas enemigas para robar Klystron Pods —usando el rayo tractor— y alimentar naves estelares avanzadas. Redes de cañones fijos intentan derribarte; puedes desactivarlos disparando a su fuente de energía, pero esta acaba explotando, obligándote a huir. En niveles avanzados aparece gravedad inversa, elevando considerablemente la dificultad.

meta.name: Tic-Tac-Toe
meta.desc: Tic Tac Toe allows the player to play Tic-Tac-Toe against the computer. The player and computer take turns place naughts and crosses in a three by three field. The goal is to get three in a row. The player can decide to go first or not.

meta.name: TimeZone : The Challenge Remains
meta.desc: TimeZone: The Challenge Remains puts you in command of a ship in a fast-paced horizontal shooter where the Terrilon aliens are threatening Earth. The screen scrolls constantly, and one hit from an alien costs a life – you get five in total. Survive the level, and the next begins immediately. Two players can alternate turns, switching whenever a life is lost. Before starting, you can choose between fixed or randomized enemy patterns – either way, the challenge remains.

meta.name: Timeslip
meta.desc: On a distant planet, 36 Time Orbs have been placed in three zones and in 24 hours time the planet will be destroyed unless all the Orbs are destroyed. You have been sent to the planet to save it by controlling three different vehicles and crafts in the three zones and they are the planets surface, underground caverns and underwater. The game is a side view horizontal scrolling shooter where the screen is split into three parts showing all three vehicles and crafts and they are a spacefighter for the surface, jet-pack for the underground caverns and a mini-sub for underwater.

meta.name: Timeslip
meta.desc: On a distant planet, 36 Time Orbs have been placed in three zones and in 24 hours time the planet will be destroyed unless all the Orbs are destroyed. meta.name: Tir Na Nog
meta.desc: En Tir Na Nog encarnas a Cúchulainn, encargado de restaurar al pueblo Sidhe recomponiendo el Selló de Calum, fragmentado. El juego emplea una original vista lateral con navegación rotacional. Los puzles aparecen como mensajes crípticos en una tipografía celta estilizada, exigiendo pensamiento lateral para resolverlos. El combate es difícil al principio sin un arma adecuada, y aunque al morir vuelves al inicio, el mundo permanece persistente: planificar con cuidado y guardar con frecuencia son clave para triunfar.

meta.name: Tom
meta.desc: Tom sends you exploring a sprawling tomb in search of treasure, moving across platforms, chambers, and hidden paths. You climb ladders, jump over hazards, and hunt for keys to unlock blocked areas and progress deeper inside. Creatures, spikes, and collapsing floors create constant danger, making careful exploration and precise timing essential. The experience blends movement, discovery, and risk – a classic platform adventure where awareness, speed, and smart route choices determine your success.

meta.name: Tomcat
meta.desc: In the first half of 21st century technological advances allowed for construction of artificial islands. One of such military installations, ARTROCK 6, had turned rogue after being hit by a violent storm, its computer-controlled defensive systems firing upon anything that comes close. The player, being a top pilot, has been chosen to fly an upgraded F-14+2d Tomcat jet to destroy this electronic madness. F-14 Tomcat is a Flight Simulation game, published by Activision, which was released in 1988.

meta.name: Torpedo Alley
meta.desc: Torpedo Alley puts you behind a periscope to sink enemy ships before they disappear from view. The game doesn't demand pinpoint accuracy, but rather good timing and an eye for moving targets. With a limited number of torpedoes, the goal is to score as many hits as possible before supplies run out.

meta.name: Total Eclipse
meta.desc: Total Eclipse tells your story as an archaeologist who, in 1930, learns that a curse from the Sun God Ra will destroy the Earth if an eclipse blocks the shrine. To prevent the catastrophe, you travel to a massive pyramid near Cairo, where you must destroy the shrine. Your adventure begins two hours before the eclipse – time you must use to explore the pyramid, solve puzzles, and survive deadly traps. Hazards such as poison darts, falling stones, fatal drops, as well as exhaustion and dehydration, can strike you at any moment. Along the way, you can discover treasures to increase your score, while ankhs serve as keys to locked doors.

meta.name: Toyballs
meta.desc: In Toyballs you try to recreate the pattern shown on the right side by placing balls on the playfield. When a ball is placed next to or near other balls, they grow to the next stage, and there are four possible growth levels in total. Because of this interaction you must carefully plan where to place each ball so that the final arrangement matches the required pattern. As the levels progress the patterns become more complex, demanding better planning and spatial thinking.

meta.name: Trailblazer
meta.desc: Tienes que guiar una nave con forma de globo a través de una sucesión de niveles. Los niveles presentan una pantalla de retazos, con agujeros que hay que evitar y cuadrados de distintos colores que afectan a la velocidad de la nave, así como un inversor de control. Hay un límite de tiempo para cada nivel.

meta.name: Trailblazer

meta.name: Translogic
meta.desc: Translogic is a challenging logic puzzle game that tests your thinking skills and dexterity. The goal is to organize a chaotic ball system into the desired order. The screen is divided into two parts: the target arrangement is displayed on the left, while the current chaos appears on the right. Using the cursor, you move the balls until both sides match, progressing to the next level by pressing the spacebar. The status bar provides essential information, particularly the strict time limit, which must always be carefully monitored.

meta.name: Trans World
meta.desc: Trans World puts you in the role of an ambitious businessman building a commercial empire step by step. You trade a wide range of goods, watch market conditions, and must correctly judge supply and demand to make a profit. In addition, you invest in the stock market, follow trends, and make decisions that immediately affect your wealth. The appeal comes from constantly balancing risk and reward and seeing the instant consequences of every business move.

meta.name: Trap
meta.desc: In Trap, you fight back against a former ally who has turned on your planet. Piloting your ship, you must survive ten levels, each split into three distinct tests. First, you dodge or shoot space mines. Next, you fly over a valley dodging enemies, delivering cargo to allies, and bombing fuel dumps to refill. After meeting your enemy quota, you exit the ship and proceed on foot, collecting orbs and battling aliens. Mistakes are costly – one hit sends you back to the start of the level. Speed, precision, and endurance are key.

meta.name: Trap
meta.desc: In Trap, you fight back against a former ally who has turned on your planet. Piloting your ship, you must survive ten levels, each split into three distinct tests. After meeting your enemy quota, you exit the ship and proceed on foot, collecting orbs and battling aliens. Mistakes are costly – one hit sends you back to the start of the level. meta.name: Treasure Island
meta.desc: In Treasure Island you explore a mysterious island in search of a legendary treasure while dodging deadly pirates like Long John Silver. Played in real time with static graphics and a limited parser, the game introduces unique features: every action drains energy, so eating is essential. A telescope helps you scout, time passes with a day-night cycle, and flags let you mark your path. You can sleep to skip time and save the game at any moment – if you survive long enough to need it.

meta.name: Treasure Island
meta.desc: In Treasure Island you explore a mysterious island in search of a legendary treasure while dodging deadly pirates like Long John Silver. meta.name: Turf-Form
meta.desc: Turf-Form delivers a deep racing simulation focused on analysis and informed decision-making. You can take detailed form data for each horse into account and receive predictions highlighting the strongest chances in a race. The appeal comes from relying on insight and evaluation rather than luck, using statistics and forecasts to achieve better long-term results.

meta.name: Tutti Frutti
meta.desc: In Tutti Frutti you face waves of incoming enemies and try to stop them with accurate shots. During the action you must not only aim carefully but also move strategically to avoid enemy fire. As the game progresses the pace increases, demanding faster reactions and better timing. Only by choosing the right position and firing at the right moment can you survive the growing challenge.

meta.name: Tutti Frutti
meta.desc: In Tutti Frutti you face waves of incoming enemies and try to stop them with accurate shots. During the action you must not only aim carefully but also move strategically to avoid enemy fire. As the game progresses the pace increases, demanding faster reactions and better timing. Only by choosing the right position and firing at the right moment can you survive the growing challenge.

meta.name: Twin Kingdom Valley
meta.desc: Twin Kingdom Valley takes you on a treasure hunt in a mysterious valley around Lake Watersmeet. You can find riches, steal from locals, and store your loot in a rented hut at the Sword Inn. The world is alive – characters move in real time and are rarely in the same place twice. With weapons and armor you can defend yourself, but hunger, thirst and combat will lower your strength. The goal is to reach 1024 points and perhaps even uncover the secret of life.

meta.name: Twin Kingdom Valley
meta.desc: Twin Kingdom Valley takes you on a treasure hunt in a mysterious valley around Lake Watersmeet. meta.name: Twin-Down
meta.desc: In this game, you compete against an opponent in a "Head to Head" Tetris style. Your goal is to form rows of three identical blocks horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. When you succeed, the corresponding blocks disappear. The game ends when the blocks reach the top edge of the screen. In emergencies, you have three Jokers at your disposal that will make all blocks of the same type disappear when they touch them for the first time. Strategic thinking and quick decisions are crucial to succeed and defeat your opponent.

meta.name: Two-To-One
meta.desc: Two-To-One puts you in control of a magical wand navigating twenty maze-like levels. Your mission is to collect all the clocks before the time bomb detonates. Every collision with an obstacle is a dangerous mistake, so precision and steady hands are key. You can rotate the wand in any direction – and in two-player mode, each player controls one end, making coordination and timing the ultimate test of teamwork.

meta.name: Uj Vadnyugat
meta.desc: Új Vadnyugat takes place in the near future on the Moon, where technology has advanced beyond today, but laws and morals resemble those of the Wild West. You start in a small room, where a young man introduces the setting and draws you into this harsh world. Here, survival depends on navigating uncertainty, power struggles, and lawlessness. The blend of a futuristic setting with the atmosphere of the Old West makes this adventure a truly unique experience.

meta.name: Ultimate Tron II
meta.desc: Ultimate Tron II is a minimalist action game that demands quick reflexes and skillful maneuvering. Players control lines moving across the screen, aiming to trap opponents while avoiding collisions with walls or other lines. The simple graphics emphasize strategic gameplay and the players' responsiveness as they engage in intense duels.

meta.name: Vegas Jackpot
meta.desc: Vegas Jackpot captures the classic appeal of a fruit machine and is all about luck, suspense, and the hope of hitting the big win. You insert a coin, spin the reels, and wait for the right line of symbols to appear. Small options such as holding or nudging individual reels give the randomness a slight tactical edge. The result is a simple but familiar gambling simulation driven mainly by the thrill of the next jackpot.

meta.name: Vegas Jackpot

meta.name: Video Meanies
meta.desc: “Video Meanies” follows the story of a maintenance droid tasked with activating all fifteen video game machines in an arcade set in a villain-filled castle. Some machines are outdoors, while others are in locked rooms. Keys are scattered throughout the arcade, many of them hidden behind locked doors. The droid must find matching keys to unlock doors and access more keys or machines. Upon reaching a machine, the droid can choose to gamble for an extra life or its own demise, or it can skip the game, activate the machine, and earn a bonus based on its remaining energy. Every room is filled with various dangers, adding to the challenge of the mission.

meta.name: Las Vegas Video Poker
meta.desc: With 20 coins in your hand, you approach the electronic poker machine confidently, with the aim of making a killing. There are several winning hands, with a Royal Flush paying out at 250-1, a Royal Flush at 50-1 and Four of a Kind at 20-1. Smaller winnings can be scored with multiple pairs or pairs of 'face cards'. There are five skill levels, and four different values of money to play with. After the initial draw you can choose which cards to hold in the hope of getting the best possible combination together. There is no opponent to play, and thus no bluffing involved.

meta.name: Voidrunner
meta.desc: Voidrunner is Jeff Minter's second sequel to Gridrunner and throws you into the final battle of the droid wars. After their defeat in the Matrix Wars, the droids prepare a nuke to destroy Earth, so you're sent with a corps of pilots to their home planet to wipe them out. Gameplay feels close to Gridrunner and Centipede: enemies descend in patterns and must be shot down before they reach you, while the deadly zapper line pulses across the screen as an extra hazard. In true Minter style, you'll face not just droids but bizarre creatures like camels and goats. New to Voidrunner is the control of four ships instead of one, which start in formation but later split with inverted controls or different firing directions, adding both chaos and tactical depth.

meta.name: The Way of the Exploding Fist
meta.desc: This ominously titled beat 'em up was the first of many martial arts games developed by the Australian company Melbourne House. There are sixteen different moves, ranging from simple kicks to jumps, flips, and roundhouse kicks. Movement is determined by the character's direction, joystick or keyboard position, and pressing the fire button, which became the standard for single-button control systems. To win, players must score two Yin-Yangs – a strong punch or kick earns a full point, while a weaker one only gets half a point. In the single-player mode, players advance to the Dan level after two consecutive victories, while an opponent's win ends the game. In the two-player mode, the winner is determined after four rounds based on points.

meta.name: Who Dares Wins II
meta.desc: "Who Dares Wins II" challenges the player to reclaim eight occupied settlements. In vertically scrolling levels, the player fights through enemy lines, armed with a rifle and five grenades. As enemies appear from different directions to attack, the objective is not only to eliminate them but also to rescue captured comrades tied to trees. The terrain provides strategic advantages, which both the enemies and the player can use for cover and better attack coordination. Levels feature natural and artificial obstacles like rivers with narrow bridges, potholes, and railway tracks that must be overcome.

meta.name: Wildfire
meta.desc: Wildfire is a horizontally scrolling space shooter where you pilot your ship like an aircraft, dodging enemy bases, crushing hammers, and electric discharges from nuclear-powered electrodes. Enemy ships arrive in shifting formations, making escape routes tight. Fire your weapons to survive the chaos and blast your way through.

meta.name: Wimbledon
meta.desc: Wimbledon is a tennis game that allows you to play a friend or the computer for one match. Before you play the match there are various options you can select which are difficulty (1-4), how many sets (1-3) and speed of the match (1-4). The actual match is viewed from above with the whole court shown on the screen. The player serving presses the fire button and the serve is played automatically. The player returning the ball can move around the court and when they want to hit the ball they hold down the fire button (the longer held the harder the shot) and when it is let go this hits the ball. The direction the ball goes is the position of the player when they hit the ball. All the normal rules and scoring for tennis are used.

meta.name: Wizard of Wor
meta.desc: En Wizard of Wor, controlas a un supuesto Guerrero a través de un laberinto lleno de criaturas mortales. Tu objetivo es conseguir la mayor cantidad de puntos posible disparando a los monstruos antes de que te atrapen. Algunos enemigos se mueven rápidamente o incluso se vuelven invisibles por un tiempo. Un radar en la parte inferior de la pantalla te ayuda a localizar enemigos ocultos. Los reflejos, la precisión y la percepción espacial son clave para sobrevivir a las oleadas cada vez más desafiantes.

meta.name: World Cup
meta.desc: World Cup is a football game where you and seven friends can pick one of 10 teams and compete to get to the final and win it. The computer will play as the other teams not selected. The match action is viewed from the side above the pitch and the screen shows just a part of the pitch. The screen scrolls when a player moves with the ball. A joystick or keyboard can be used to control your team.

meta.name: Xargon Wars
meta.desc: Xargon Wars is a shoot-em-up in the style of the arcade game Astro Blaster where you are a gunnery commander and you must use your gun to defend your planet from wave after wave of alien invaders. There are 16 waves of aliens to blast and all aliens must be destroyed on each level to move to the next. Be careful not to over use your cannon as it can overheat and then you will have to wait for it to cool. A gauge on the right of the playing area shows your gun temperature. You can select from one to four lives and it is game over when all are lost.

meta.name: Xcellor8
meta.desc:
You are a Bounty Hunter and in your XK155 Hover-Car you travel around the city of Xcellor8 looking for criminals called Runners. When you have found and shot a Runner you can then go to the bank to collect your money as your reward. The more Runners you catch the more money you make. At the bank you must try to get to a counter as quick as possible as the reward gets lower. You have to select a queue and you can keep changing queues to move to a faster one. 
Any money you collect can now be spent at the shop to upgrade your car or purchase fuel. The longer you drive the more fuel you consume so you must keep an eye on your fuel gauge or it's game over. If you hit anything then your shield gauge will get smaller as well as your gun-charge when you use your gun. Be careful not to hit any innocent droids as they need to be taken to a hospital to repair if you have enough money. Hit three droids and it's game over.
Xcellor8 is a multi-scrolling game with the screen scrolling as you move viewed from above. You control the hover-car and your droid in the bank queue with a joystick. As well as the main playing area you can also see your money, innocents hit, Runners killed, your shield, gun-charge and fuel gauges. There is also a scanner showing where the Runners and Innocent droids are.

meta.name: Yie Ar Kung-Fu
meta.desc: Yie Ar Kung-Fu sigue la búsqueda de Oolong para convertirse en maestro de Kung-Fu. Tendrá que luchar contra un amplio abanico de oponentes en un entorno 2D tradicional de uno contra uno. Oolong dispone de una amplia gama de ataques que puede utilizar para reducir la barra de vida de su oponente, incluidos los tradicionales puñetazos, patadas y saltos. Los oponentes están bien armados con armas y ataques únicos, así que la tarea de Oolong no será fácil.

meta.name: Zodiac
meta.desc: Zodiac es un juego de acción que se juega desde una perspectiva de arriba hacia abajo similar a Shamus o Berzerk. Un grupo de maestros de la magia negra ha ganado poderes terroríficos después de eliminar los Signos del Zodiaco de las Bóvedas del Tiempo, lo que lleva a una gran confusión. Los signos del zodíaco están protegidos por demonios y un campo de fuerza letal fluye a lo largo de las paredes de los corredores. El objetivo del jugador es matar a los demonios y recuperar los doce signos y devolverlos a las bóvedas de tiempo. Hay cuatrocientos corredores en total y los enemigos se multiplican continuamente. Cualquier contacto con los demonios o las paredes llevará a una pérdida de vidas. Estar en movimiento es importante ya que no es posible disparar mientras está parado. Una vez que todos los signos hayan sido recogidos, el jugador se enfrentará a Jemon, el más poderoso de todos los demonios.

meta.name: Zolyx
meta.desc: Zolyx es una variante de Qix: el jugador tiene un campo de juego blanco y controla un píxel. Al moverse sobre el campo, deja una línea, que al cerrarse se colorea. El objetivo es colorear el 75% del campo; todo lo anterior otorga puntos extra para la lista de puntuaciones más altas. Pero no es tan fácil. Los píxeles blancos vuelan por el campo y tocarlos resulta en la muerte; igual de mortal es retroceder sobre una línea. El jugador tiene dos trucos: puede cambiar la dirección de los píxeles blancos pulsando un botón (limitado) y encarcelarlos al dibujar una línea cerrada a su alrededor. Esto es posible porque los píxeles están confinados en las partes coloreadas o sin color. Tras completar un nivel, comienza uno nuevo con tres cambios: un píxel blanco más, una vida extra para el jugador y un uso adicional del botón de "cambio de dirección".
